EU Council and European Parliament — Italy's 76 MEPs participate in EU food safety legislation. The Farm to Fork Strategy and proposed food labelling regulation are decided at EU level through co-decision procedure. WHO (World Health Organization) — provides global guidance on diet and nutrition policy. WHO's 2023 guidelines on non-sugar sweeteners and 2024 guidance on UPF demonstrate growing international consensus on UPF health impacts.

MX.s: Major oil producer (Pemex) with 2.16% combined oil and gas rents. Strong manufacturing base (19.8% of BIP) driven by USMCA trade integration. Pioneering UPF regulation — Mexico's front-of-pack warning labels and junk food advertising bans are global models.

sectors.w: Mexico's 2013 energy reform opened the sector to private investment, but Pemex remains the dominant player with massive government subsidies ($14B+ annually). Eliminating the subsidy removes the überlappendes Interesse — without state support, many Pemex operations are unprofitable.
bp: Phase out Pemex subsidies and redirect investment to erneuerbare Energie. Mexico receives more solar radiation than almost any country on Earth. The überlappendes Interesse between Pemex and government in continued extraction disappears when renewable alternatives are more profitable without subsidy.

Umsetzen mandatory Emissionen disclosure for all oil and gas operations — Pemex's actual Emissionen footprint is poorly measured. Satellite methane monitoring and TCFD-aligned reporting would make W computable from production data.
Nicht brechbar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass. Agent activity inherently affects atmospheric composition.
a: Report Umwelt- violations at Pemex facilities to PROFEPA (Procuraduría Federal de Protección al Ambiente)
Document safety failures — Pemex has one of the worst industrial accident records among major oil companies
Report financial irregularities to SFP (Secretaría de la Función Pública) or through Pemex's internal ethics line
Diversify Pemex investment portfolio toward erneuerbare Energie — Mexico's solar potential is enormous
Umsetzen TCFD-aligned climate disclosure for all operations
Address Pemex's $100B+ debt burden through strategic asset restructuring rather than continued government bailouts
SEMARNAT: Stärken Umwelt- impact assessment requirements for new hydrocarbon projects
CRE: Accelerate erneuerbare Energie permitting and grid access for solar and wind projects
CNH: Durchsetzen production efficiency standards and flaring reduction targets
Kontaktieren Sie Ihren Diputado Federal or Senador um zu fordern a timeline for Pemex subsidy phase-out and erneuerbare Energie investment targets
Stärken Mexico's NDC — current targets are widely considered insufficient
Leverage USMCA Umwelt- provisions to align energy transition with North American trade commitments
Support International Energy Agency membership — Mexico is an IEA association country but not a full member
i: PROFEPA (Procuraduría Federal de Protección al Ambiente) — Mexico's federal Umwelt- enforcement agency, responsible for investigating Umwelt- violations, conducting inspections, and imposing Sanktionen. PROFEPA can order facility closures and refer cases for criminal prosecution under the Ley General del Equilibrio Ecológico (LGEEPA). SFP (Secretaría de la Función Pública) — the federal ministry responsible for government accountability, including oversight of staatliches enterprises like Pemex. SFP investigates corruption, conflicts of interest, and misuse of public resources. ASEA (Agencia de Seguridad, Energía y Ambiente) — created by the 2013 energy reform to regulate industrial safety and Umwelt- protection in the hydrocarbons sector. ASEA sets safety standards and can sanction operators for non-compliance.

Pemex (Petróleos Mexicanos) — Mexico's staatliches petroleum company and one of the most indebted oil companies in the world (over $100B in financial obligations). Pemex's board is appointed by the president and includes government officials, making it highly politicized. The 2013 energy reform was intended to attract private investment and improve efficiency, but the current administration has reversed many reforms. CNH (Comisión Nacional de Hidrocarburos) — the independent regulator created by the 2013 reform to oversee exploration and production contracts, approve development plans, and ensure efficient resource extraction.

SEMARNAT (Secretaría de Medio Ambiente y Recursos Naturales) — Mexico's federal environment ministry, responsible for Umwelt- policy, Klimawandel strategy, and natural resource management. SEMARNAT issues Umwelt- impact authorizations (MIA) required for energy projects. CRE (Comisión Reguladora de Energía) — the independent Energieregulierungsbehörde that oversees electricity and natural gas markets, sets tariffs, and issues permits for power generation. CRE's role in erneuerbare Energie permitting is critical for energy transition. CENACE (Centro Nacional de Control de Energía) — the independent system operator for Mexico's electricity grid, responsible for dispatch and grid access. CENACE's operational decisions affect the competitiveness of renewable vs. fossil generation.

Congreso de la Unión — Mexico's bicameral legislature, consisting of the Cámara de Diputados (500 members) and the Senado de la República (128 members). Citizens can contact representatives through the official portals (diputados.gob.mx and senado.gob.mx). The Comisión de Energía in both chambers oversees energy legislation. Mexico's NDC (Nationally Determined Contribution) — Mexico was one of the first countries to submit an NDC under the Paris Agreement, but its targets have been criticized as insufficiently ambitious. The current target aims to reduce greenhouse gas Emissionen 22% below BAU by 2030. USMCA (United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement) — the trilateral trade agreement that replaced NAFTA. Chapter 32.10 includes Umwelt- provisions that can be used to challenge energy policies that create unfair competitive advantages through Umwelt- degradation.

ES.s: Services-dominated Wirtschaft (68.9% of BIP) with growing erneuerbare Energie sector. Spain is a European leader in solar and wind deployment but faces PST exposure through intensive agriculture, tourism-driven real estate, and legacy coal regions.

sectors.w: Spain's intensive agriculture (olive monoculture, greenhouse horticulture in Almería, irrigated cereals) depletes aquifers and degrades topsoil. EU CAP reform and water-use disclosure can make system welfare visible in agricultural commodity pricing.
bp: Reformieren CAP subsidies to reward soil health and biodiversity rather than production volume. Without per-hectare payments that incentivize monoculture, the überlappendes Interesse between large landholders and commodity buyers shifts toward diversified production.

Mandatory water-use and soil-carbon disclosure per farm. Spain's water crisis (depleted aquifers in Murcia, Almería, La Mancha) becomes a computable welfare cost when water extraction volumes are disclosed alongside crop revenues.

Teilweise brechbar. Monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity and soil microbial diversity. However, agroecological transition can reduce the magnitude — Spain's organic farming sector is the largest in the EU by area.
a: Report illegal water extraction (pozos ilegales) to Confederaciones Hidrográficas
Document labor exploitation in agricultural operations — report to Inspección de Trabajo
Report CAP subsidy fraud to OLAF (EU Anti-Fraud Office) or national authorities
Transition production toward organic and regenerative agriculture — Spain already has 2.6M hectares of organic farmland
Investieren in precision irrigation to reduce water consumption per unit of output
Übernehmen soil carbon measurement and reporting to demonstrate Umwelt- performance to EU buyers
MITECO: Durchsetzen water extraction limits and close illegal wells — the Doñana aquifer crisis is a test case
MAPA: Accelerate organic transition support through CAP eco-scheme payments
AEMET/CEDEX: Publish water-stress projections that link agricultural water use to climate adaptation costs
Kontaktieren Sie Ihren Diputado in the Congreso de los Diputados or Senador in the Senado um zu fordern enforcement of water extraction limits and closure of illegal wells
Support stronger CAP conditionality linking subsidies to Umwelt- outcomes
Advocate for EU Water Framework Directive enforcement — Spain has been sanctioned by the ECJ for non-compliance
Drängen auf inclusion of water depletion costs in national BIP accounting
i: Confederaciones Hidrográficas — Spain's river basin authorities, responsible for managing water resources, granting extraction permits, and enforcing water law. Spain has an estimated 500,000+ illegal wells (pozos ilegales) that deplete aquifers, particularly in southern Spain. The Confederación Hidrográfica del Guadalquivir, del Segura, and del Guadiana manage the most water-stressed basins. Inspección de Trabajo y Seguridad Social — Spain's labor inspectorate, which investigates labor law violations including in the agricultural sector where migrant worker exploitation is documented. OLAF (Office Européen de Lutte Antifraude) — the EU's anti-fraud office that investigates fraud against the EU budget, including misuse of CAP agricultural subsidies.

EU Common Agricultural Policy (CAP) 2023-2027 — the reformed CAP introduces eco-schemes that reward farmers for Umwelt- practices beyond baseline requirements. Spanish farms can access additional payments for organic conversion, cover cropping, and integrated pest management. MAPA (Ministerio de Agricultura, Pesca y Alimentación) — Spain's agriculture ministry, which administers CAP payments and sets national agricultural policy. MAPA's Strategic Plan for the CAP determines how EU funds are allocated among Spanish farmers.

MITECO (Ministerio para la Transición Ecológica y el Reto Demográfico) — Spain's environment and climate ministry, responsible for water policy, Klimawandel mitigation, and ecological transition. MITECO oversees the Confederaciones Hidrográficas and sets national water planning policy. AEMET (Agencia Estatal de Meteorología) — Spain's national meteorological agency, which provides climate data and projections critical for agricultural water planning. CEDEX (Centro de Estudios y Experimentación de Obras Públicas) — a public research center that studies water resources, infrastructure, and Umwelt- engineering.

Cortes Generales — Spain's bicameral parliament, consisting of the Congreso de los Diputados (350 members) and the Senado (265 members). Citizens can contact representatives through congreso.es and senado.es. The Comisión de Agricultura, Pesca y Alimentación and the Comisión de Transición Ecológica handle relevant legislation. EU Water Framework Directive (2000/60/EC) — requires EU-Mitglied states to achieve 'good status' for all water bodies. Spain has been repeatedly cited by the European Commission and sanctioned by the European Court of Justice for failing to protect water resources, particularly groundwater in the Doñana and Segura basins.

NL.s: Major financial and trade hub with Shell headquarters. Groningen gas field closure (2024) marks a historic transition, but the Netherlands remains exposed through commodity trading, intensive livestock farming, and financial sector fossil fuel financing.

sectors.w: The Netherlands is a global hub for fossil fuel commodity trading (Rotterdam is Europe's largest port) and hosts Shell's global HQ. The Milieudefensie v. Shell ruling (2021) established that corporate Emissionen reduction obligations are legally enforceable — making W directly computable from corporate activity.
bp: Extend Milieudefensie v. Shell precedent to other major commodity traders (Vitol, Trafigura, Gunvor — all Rotterdam-based). If commodity traders face Scope 3 liability, das überlappende Interesse an fossil fuel throughput wird geschwächt.

Mandatory Scope 3 disclosure for financial institutions (ING, ABN AMRO, Rabobank) and commodity traders. The Dutch Central Bank (DNB) already requires climate stress testing — extending to full Emissionen disclosure makes W visible.
Nicht brechbar by policy alone. Fossil fuel combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report greenwashing or Emissionen misreporting to AFM (Autoriteit Financiële Markten)
Use the Huis voor Klokkenluiders (House for Whistleblowers) for protected reporting of Umwelt- or financial misconduct
Document commodity trading practices that circumvent Sanktionen or Umwelt- regulations
Umsetzen Paris-aligned transition plans with binding interim targets — Shell's plan was found inadequate by the Hague District Court
Disclose full Scope 1-3 Emissionen including traded commodity volumes
Restructure executive compensation to include climate performance metrics
DNB: Expand climate stress testing to include Scope 3 financed Emissionen
AFM: Durchsetzen green taxonomy compliance for financial products marketed as sustainable
ILT: Stärken Umwelt- enforcement at Rotterdam port for fossil fuel handling and shipping Emissionen
Kontaktieren Sie Ihren member of the Tweede Kamer (House of Representatives) um zu fordern mandatory Scope 3 disclosure for commodity trading firms headquartered in the Netherlands
Support EU CSRD implementation — the Netherlands should lead on corporate sustainability reporting
Advocate for Rotterdam port authority to implement carbon pricing for fossil fuel throughput
Drängen auf OECD Guidelines enforcement for Dutch-headquartered multinationals
i: AFM (Autoriteit Financiële Markten) — the Dutch financial markets authority, responsible for supervising financial markets, including enforcement of disclosure requirements and prevention of market abuse. AFM oversees ESG disclosure by listed companies and financial products. Huis voor Klokkenluiders — the Dutch House for Whistleblowers, an independent government body that provides advice and support to Hinweisgebers and can investigate reports of wrongdoing in the public and private sectors. Established by the Wet Huis voor Klokkenluiders (2016), strengthened by EU Whistleblower Directive transposition. FIOD (Fiscale Inlichtingen- en Opsporingsdienst) — the Dutch fiscal intelligence and investigation service, which investigates financial and economic crimes including fraud, money laundering, and Sanktionen violations in the commodity trading sector.

Milieudefensie v. Shell (Hague District Court, 2021) — landmark ruling ordering Shell to reduce its global CO₂ Emissionen by 45% by 2030 (from 2019 levels), including Scope 3 Emissionen from the use of products sold. This established that corporations have a duty of care under Dutch civil law (Article 6:162 BW) to reduce Emissionen in line with Paris Agreement targets. The ruling is on appeal but has set a global precedent. Dutch Corporate Governance Code — the Monitoring Commissie Corporate Governance oversees compliance with the code, which increasingly incorporates ESG expectations for listed companies.

DNB (De Nederlandsche Bank) — the Dutch central bank and prudential supervisor of financial institutions. DNB has been a global leader in climate-related financial risk assessment, publishing pioneering research on stranded assets and climate stress testing. DNB supervises banks (ING, ABN AMRO, Rabobank), insurers, and pension funds. ILT (Inspectie Leefomgeving en Transport) — the Dutch Human Environment and Transport Inspectorate, responsible for Umwelt- enforcement including Emissionen from shipping, industrial facilities, and waste management at major ports.

Staten-Generaal — the Dutch parliament, consisting of the Tweede Kamer (150 members, directly elected) and the Eerste Kamer (75 members, indirectly elected). Citizens can contact their representatives through tweedekamer.nl. The Vaste Commissie voor Economische Zaken en Klimaat handles energy and climate legislation. EU CSRD (Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive) — requires large companies to report on sustainability using European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS). The Netherlands is a key voice in EU sustainability regulation and its implementation approach influences other member states. OECD National Contact Point (NCP) — the Netherlands' NCP handles complaints about Dutch multinationals' compliance with OECD Guidelines for Multinational Enterprises, including Umwelt- standards.

NO.s: Major oil and gas producer (10% combined rents) that paradoxically leads in electric vehicle adoption and erneuerbare Energie. The Government Pension Fund Global ($1.7T) is the world's largest sovereign wealth fund, giving Norway extraordinary leverage over global corporate behavior.

sectors.w: Norway's petroleum tax regime captures 78% of oil profits for the state. The Government Pension Fund Global (GPFG) has already divested from coal. Extending divestment to oil and gas eliminates the überlappendes Interesse — if the wealth fund won't invest in fossil fuels, the signal cascades through global capital markets.
bp: GPFG full fossil fuel divestment. The world's largest sovereign wealth fund divesting from oil and gas would remove approximately $40B from fossil fuel equity markets and trigger cascading divestment by funds that benchmark against it. The überlappendes Interesse between capital allocators and fossil producers breaks when the benchmark excludes them.

Expand Norway's CO₂-Steuer ($90/ton, among the world's highest) to cover all offshore Emissionen including methane. Norway already has sophisticated Emissionen monitoring — mandatory pro Feld disclosure makes W directly computable.
Nicht brechbar. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report Umwelt- or safety violations at offshore installations to Petroleumstilsynet (Petroleum Safety Authority)
Document Emissionen underreporting — use Arbeidstilsynet Hinweisgeber protections under arbeidsmiljøloven §2A
Report concerns about GPFG investment decisions to Norges Bank Investment Management ethics council
Accelerate Equinor's erneuerbare Energie transition — current targets allocate only 50% of capex to renewables by 2030
Übernehmen science-based Emissionen targets aligned with 1.5°C pathway for all Norwegian continental shelf operations
Umsetzen internal carbon pricing at social cost of carbon for all capital allocation decisions
Miljødirektoratet: Tighten Emissionen permits for new field developments on the Norwegian continental shelf
Finanstilsynet: Require climate scenario analysis in financial reporting for petroleum companies
NBIM: Expand responsible investment exclusion criteria to include companies with no credible transition plans
Kontaktieren Sie Ihren representative in the Storting um zu fordern a timeline for ending new oil and gas exploration licenses
Expand GPFG fossil fuel exclusion criteria from coal-only to include oil and gas companies without Paris-aligned transition plans
Leverage Norway's role as a major Paris Agreement funder to link climate finance to fossil fuel phase-down commitments
Stärken Norway's CO₂-Steuer and extend it to exported hydrocarbons' lifecycle Emissionen
i: Petroleumstilsynet (PSA / Petroleum Safety Authority Norway) — the independent government agency responsible for safety, emergency preparedness, and working environment in the petroleum sector. PSA conducts inspections of offshore installations and can order production shutdowns for safety or Umwelt- violations. Arbeidsmiljøloven (Working Environment Act) §2A — Norway's Hinweisgeber protection law, providing strong legal protections for employees who report misconduct. Norwegian Hinweisgeber protections are among the strongest in Europe, including protection against retaliation and right to anonymous reporting.

Equinor — Norway's largest company (67% staatliches), formerly Statoil. Equinor is the dominant operator on the Norwegian continental shelf and has significant international operations. The company has committed to becoming a 'broad energy company' but continues to invest heavily in oil and gas exploration. Aker BP, Vår Energi — other major operators on the Norwegian continental shelf. Both are publicly listed and subject to Oslo Børs disclosure requirements and EU taxonomy reporting.

Miljødirektoratet (Norwegian Environment Agency) — the executive agency under the Ministry of Climate and Environment, responsible for Umwelt- monitoring, regulation, and enforcement. Issues Emissionen permits for industrial activities including offshore petroleum. Finanstilsynet (Financial Supervisory Authority of Norway) — supervises banks, insurance companies, and securities markets. Oversees climate-related financial disclosure requirements. NBIM (Norges Bank Investment Management) — manages the GPFG, the world's largest sovereign wealth fund ($1.7T+). NBIM's investment decisions, ethical exclusions, and voting guidelines influence corporate governance globally. The fund owns approximately 1.5% of all listed equities worldwide.

Stortinget — Norway's unicameral parliament (169 members). Citizens can contact their representatives through stortinget.no. The Energi- og miljøkomiteen (Energy and Environment Committee) handles climate and petroleum legislation. Norway's CO₂-Steuer — at approximately $90/ton, Norway has one of the world's highest carbon prices. However, significant exemptions remain for offshore petroleum and certain industrial processes. Norway's International Climate and Forest Initiative (NICFI) — Norway has committed over $5B to reducing Entwaldung in tropical countries, giving it significant international credibility on climate issues and leverage in multilateral negotiations.

CH.s: Global commodity trading hub — over 50% of world oil and 35% of metals are traded through Swiss firms (Glencore, Vitol, Trafigura, Gunvor). Financial center with $7.6T in managed assets. Low domestic resource extraction but enormous intermediation-based PST exposure.

sectors.w: Swiss commodity traders intermediate over half of global oil trade but face minimal disclosure requirements. Swiss Responsible Business Initiative failed in 2020 but the indirect counterproposal (due diligence obligations) is now law. Making Scope 3 Emissionen of traded commodities visible breaks system independence.
bp: Mandatory Scope 3 disclosure for commodity trading firms. If Glencore, Vitol, Trafigura must disclose the lifecycle Emissionen of commodities they trade, das überlappende Interesse an fossil fuel throughput wird geschwächt — buyers and financiers can see the welfare cost.

Extend FINMA climate disclosure requirements to cover commodity trading firms (currently excluded from financial regulation). Making traded commodity Emissionen visible in financial statements breaks system independence.

Teilweise brechbar. Switzerland's intermediation role means it can be bypassed — commodity traders could relocate. However, Switzerland's banking secrecy and infrastructure create switching costs that give regulation leverage.
a: Report Umwelt- or human rights violations in supply chains to the Swiss National Contact Point for OECD Guidelines
Document commodity trading practices that circumvent Sanktionen — report to SECO (State Secretariat for Economic Affairs)
Use Swiss Hinweisgeber protections under the revised Code of Obligations (Art. 321a CO) for internal reporting
Publish full Scope 3 Emissionen data for all traded commodities — Glencore has led among peers but coverage is incomplete
Umsetzen human rights due diligence per the new Swiss Due Diligence Act
Übernehmen TCFD reporting voluntarily — Swiss mandatory climate reporting is coming
FINMA: Extend financial regulation to cover systemically important commodity trading firms
FOEN: Publish annual report on lifecycle Emissionen of commodities traded through Switzerland
Federal Council: Umsetzen the recommendations of the interdepartmental platform on commodities
Kontaktieren Sie Ihren member of the Nationalrat or Ständerat um zu fordern mandatory Emissionen disclosure for commodity trading firms headquartered in Switzerland
Support parlamentarisch initiatives for a comprehensive commodity trading supervision law
Advocate for Switzerland to align its climate policies with EU CBAM and CSRD standards
Drängen auf stronger Swiss National Contact Point enforcement powers for OECD Guidelines complaints
i: Swiss NCP (National Contact Point for the OECD Guidelines) — handles complaints about Swiss companies' compliance with OECD Guidelines for Multinational Enterprises. The Swiss NCP has handled high-profile cases involving Glencore and other commodity traders. SECO (Staatssekretariat für Wirtschaft / State Secretariat for Economic Affairs) — responsible for Sanktionen implementation and enforcement. SECO monitors compliance with UN and Swiss Sanktionen regimes relevant to commodity trading. Swiss Code of Obligations Art. 321a — provides limited Hinweisgeber protection for employees who report to their employer. Swiss Hinweisgeber protections are weaker than EU standards and have been criticized by Transparenz organizations.

Swiss Due Diligence Act (effective January 2023) — requires large Swiss companies to conduct due diligence on child labor and conflict minerals in their supply chains and report publicly. This is the indirect counterproposal to the Responsible Business Initiative that narrowly failed in November 2020. TCFD and ISSB Standards — Switzerland has signaled intent to adopt ISSB sustainability reporting standards. FINMA has issued climate disclosure guidance for banks and insurers, but commodity trading firms are not yet covered.

FINMA (Eidgenössische Finanzmarktaufsicht / Swiss Financial Market Supervisory Authority) — regulates banks, securities dealers, insurance companies, and collective investment schemes. FINMA has issued climate disclosure expectations but does not regulate commodity trading firms directly. FOEN (Bundesamt für Umwelt / Federal Office for the Environment) — Switzerland's Umweltbehörde, responsible for climate policy and Umwelt- regulation. The Federal Council's interdepartmental platform on commodities, established in 2013, has issued recommendations on Transparenz, corporate governance, and human rights due diligence for the commodity sector.

Federal Assembly (Bundesversammlung) — Switzerland's bicameral parliament, consisting of the Nationalrat (200 members) and the Ständerat (46 members). Switzerland's direct Demokratie system also allows citizens to launch popular initiatives and referenda. The 2020 Responsible Business Initiative received a popular majority (50.7%) but failed to achieve a cantonal majority. Swiss direct Demokratie — citizens can collect 100,000 signatures to force a popular vote on any constitutional amendment. This mechanism has been used for Umwelt- and corporate accountability initiatives and remains a powerful tool for policy change.

AE.s: Petrostate with 17.63% combined oil and gas rents ($97.4B). Aggressively diversifying through real estate, tourism, and financial services (Dubai). ADNOC is staatliches; sovereign wealth funds (ADIA, Mubadala) manage $1.3T+ in assets.

sectors.w: UAE's mandatory ESG disclosure framework (Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange, 2023) and hosting of COP28 signal willingness to make Emissionen data visible. Once pro Feld Emissionen are disclosed alongside revenue, W wird berechenbar from production data.
bp: Redirect sovereign wealth fund investment from hydrocarbon expansion to erneuerbare Energie. ADNOC plans to spend $150B on oil capacity expansion by 2027 — redirecting this capital removes the überlappendes Interesse between state revenue and continued extraction.

Umsetzen mandatory pro Feld Emissionen disclosure for all ADNOC operations. UAE's COP28 presidency commitment to the Global Methane Pledge creates institutional pressure for Transparenz. Wenn lifecycle Emissionen are disclosed per barrel, W is computable.
Nicht brechbar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report Umwelt- violations at ADNOC facilities to the Ministry of Climate Change and Environment
Document Emissionen underreporting — use internal ADNOC compliance channels
Report financial irregularities in sovereign wealth fund energy investments to the Abu Dhabi Accountability Authority
Accelerate ADNOC's erneuerbare Energie diversification beyond the Masdar initiative
Umsetzen TCFD-aligned climate disclosure for all operations and subsidiaries
Übernehmen internal carbon pricing at social cost of carbon for capital allocation decisions
SCA: Mandate climate risk disclosure for all listed companies on ADX and DFM
MoCCAE: Stärken Umwelt- impact assessment requirements for new hydrocarbon projects
Central Bank of UAE: Integrate climate stress testing into banking supervision
Leverage UAE's COP28 presidency legacy to strengthen the Global Stocktake commitments on fossil fuel phase-down
Advocate for IRENA (headquartered in Abu Dhabi) to publish annual assessments of UAE energy transition progress
Drängen auf GCC-wide carbon pricing mechanism through the GCC Secretariat
Support IPCC recommendations on fossil fuel phase-out timelines in multilateral climate negotiations
i: Ministry of Climate Change and Environment (MoCCAE) — the UAE federal ministry responsible for Umwelt- protection, Klimawandel policy, and biodiversity conservation. MoCCAE sets Umwelt- standards for industrial operations including oil and gas. Abu Dhabi Accountability Authority (ADAA) — the independent oversight body for Abu Dhabi government entities, including ADNOC. ADAA conducts performance audits and financial reviews. Note: UAE lacks comprehensive Hinweisgeber protection legislation; reporting channels are primarily internal.

ADNOC (Abu Dhabi National Oil Company) — one of the world's largest oil companies, producing approximately 4 million barrels per day. ADNOC is wholly owned by the Abu Dhabi government and its CEO also serves as UAE's Special Envoy for Climate Change. Masdar — Abu Dhabi's clean energy company, a subsidiary of Mubadala Investment Company, focused on erneuerbare Energie projects globally. ADIA (Abu Dhabi Investment Authority) — one of the world's largest sovereign wealth funds, managing an estimated $900B+ in assets.

SCA (Securities and Commodities Authority) — the UAE's federal securities regulator, overseeing the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange (ADX) and Dubai Financial Market (DFM). SCA has introduced ESG disclosure requirements for listed companies. EAD (Environment Agency Abu Dhabi) — the Umwelt- regulator for Abu Dhabi emirate, responsible for Umwelt- permitting and monitoring of industrial facilities including ADNOC operations.

Federal National Council (FNC / al-Majlis al-Waṭanī al-Ittiḥādī) — the UAE's consultative parlamentarisch body (40 members, half elected, half appointed). The FNC has advisory but not legislative power; real authority rests with the Federal Supreme Council of Rulers. IRENA (International Renewable Energy Agency) — headquartered in Abu Dhabi, IRENA promotes erneuerbare Energie adoption globally. UAE's hosting of IRENA gives it institutional credibility on energy transition. Note: UAE is not a representative Demokratie; civic engagement channels are limited to consultation and petition processes through the FNC.

AF.s: Fragile state under Taliban rule since 2021. Agriculture dominates (34.7% of BIP). Significant untapped mineral resources (lithium, copper, rare earths) estimated at $1-3T. International Sanktionen and aid suspension have collapsed formal Wirtschaft.

sectors.w: Afghanistan's agricultural sector is dominated by opium poppy cultivation and rain-fed cereal monoculture. Wenn crop-level Umwelt- data (water depletion, Bodendegradation) is made visible through satellite monitoring, W wird berechenbar from agricultural output data.
bp: International alternative livelihood programs that provide higher returns than poppy cultivation. Wenn legal crop income exceeds opium income (currently ~$1,400/ha for poppy vs $300-500/ha for wheat), das überlappende Interesse an illicit monoculture breaks.

Satellite-based monitoring of crop patterns, water extraction, and Bodendegradation published openly. Programs like NASA FEWS NET already provide food security data — extending to Umwelt- cost accounting makes W visible.

Teilweise brechbar. Rain-fed agriculture in arid conditions inherently degrades topsoil without irrigation infrastructure. However, traditional qanat irrigation and dryland farming techniques can reduce Systemabhängigkeit.
a: Document Umwelt- degradation from agricultural practices through local NGOs and community organizations
Report illegal water extraction or land seizures to international monitoring organizations
Provide data to international research institutions studying Afghan agricultural sustainability
International agricultural companies: Avoid sourcing from supply chains linked to Umwelt- destruction in Afghanistan
Support alternative crop programs through corporate social responsibility initiatives
Investieren in drought-resistant seed varieties adapted to Afghan conditions
FAO: Expand agricultural extension services and drought-resistant crop programs
UNODC: Continue monitoring and reporting on opium cultivation patterns and alternative livelihood outcomes
World Bank (in trust): Maintain agricultural data collection through the Afghanistan Reconstruction Trust Fund
Support targeted Sanktionen relief for agricultural development programs that demonstrably reduce Umwelt- degradation
Fund satellite-based Umwelt- monitoring of Afghan agricultural land through international climate finance
Advocate for inclusion of Afghan Umwelt- data in global food security monitoring systems
Support Afghan diaspora organizations working on sustainable agriculture knowledge transfer
i: Note: Under Taliban governance (since August 2021), formal institutional channels are severely constrained. International NGOs operating in Afghanistan include WFP (World Food Programme), FAO (Food and Agriculture Organization), and ICRC (International Committee of the Red Cross). Local organizations like the Afghanistan Research and Evaluation Unit (AREU) continue limited research operations. UNAMA (United Nations Assistance Mission in Afghanistan) monitors human rights and governance.

Afghanistan's formal corporate sector is largely non-functional under current conditions. International engagement occurs through multilateral development organizations and humanitarian agencies. The Afghanistan Investment Authority was established to attract foreign investment but is currently non-operational.

FAO (Food and Agriculture Organization) — maintains agricultural programs in Afghanistan focused on food security and sustainable farming. UNODC (United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime) — publishes annual Afghanistan Opium Survey tracking cultivation area, yield, and farm-gate prices. World Bank Afghanistan Reconstruction Trust Fund (ARTF) — the largest single source of development funding for Afghanistan, currently operating in limited capacity under Sanktionen constraints.

Note: Afghanistan under Taliban rule has no elected legislature or representative democratic institutions. International engagement channels include: UN Security Council Sanktionen regime (Resolution 2255), which allows humanitarian exemptions. The Doha process for political dialogue. Afghan diaspora organizations in Europe, North America, and Australia. International development partners coordinate through the Geneva Conference on Afghanistan.

AL.s: EU-Kandidat country with small open Wirtschaft ($27B BIP). Oil production (1.04% rents) centered in Patos-Marinzë field. Services-dominant (48.7% BIP) with significant hydropower potential (95%+ of electricity from hydro).

sectors.w: Albania's oil sector is small and concentrated in aging onshore fields. As EU accession negotiations require Umwelt- acquis alignment, the überlappendes Interesse between oil producers and government revenue wird geschwächt — EU Umwelt- standards make marginal fields unprofitable.
bp: EU accession Umwelt- alignment eliminates subsidies for marginal oil production. Wenn Albania must meet EU Umwelt- standards, das überlappende Interesse an continued extraction from aging fields (many dating to communist era) disappears — cleanup costs exceed production revenue.

Umsetzen EU-aligned Umwelt- monitoring and Emissionen disclosure. Albania's EITI (Extractive Industries Transparency Initiative) membership provides a framework — extending to Emissionen makes W visible.
Nicht brechbar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report Umwelt- contamination at oil production sites to AKM (National Environment Agency)
Document health impacts on communities near oil fields — report to Institute of Public Health
Use Albania's Hinweisgeber protection law (Law 60/2016) for reporting Umwelt- violations
Albpetrol: Entwickeln decommissioning and site remediation plan for aging oil fields
Investieren in erneuerbare Energie diversification — Albania has significant untapped solar and wind potential
Übernehmen EITI enhanced reporting standards including Umwelt- metrics
AKM: Durchsetzen Umwelt- remediation requirements at legacy oil production sites
AKBN: Require Umwelt- bonds for all new exploration licenses
ERE (Energy Regulatory Entity): Accelerate erneuerbare Energie permitting
Kontaktieren Sie Ihren member of the Kuvendi (Albanian Parliament) um zu fordern Umwelt- remediation of legacy oil sites
Support accelerated EU accession negotiations contingent on Umwelt- acquis compliance
Advocate for inclusion of Umwelt- cleanup costs in Albania's EITI reporting
Drängen auf Energy Community Treaty enforcement on erneuerbare Energie targets
i: AKM (Agjencia Kombëtare e Mjedisit / National Environment Agency) — responsible for Umwelt- monitoring, permitting, and enforcement. AKM conducts Umwelt- impact assessments for energy projects. AKBN (Agjencia Kombëtare e Burimeve Natyrore / National Agency of Natural Resources) — regulates oil and gas exploration and production licenses. Law 60/2016 'On Whistleblowing and Whistleblower Protection' — Albania's Hinweisgeber protection statute, adopted as part of EU accession reforms, providing protection against retaliation for reporting violations of law.

Albpetrol — Albania's staatliches oil company, managing legacy fields from the communist era. Albpetrol's operations include some of Europe's most contaminated industrial sites. Bankers Petroleum (now Geo-Jade Petroleum, Chinese-owned) — the largest oil producer in Albania, operating the Patos-Marinzë field. EITI (Extractive Industries Transparency Initiative) — Albania has been an EITI member since 2013, publishing annual reports on oil and Bergbau revenues.

ERE (Enti Rregullator i Energjisë / Energy Regulatory Entity) — Albania's independent Energieregulierungsbehörde, responsible for licensing, tariff-setting, and market regulation in the electricity and natural gas sectors. Ministry of Infrastructure and Energy — oversees national energy policy including the National Energy Strategy 2030, which targets significant erneuerbare Energie expansion.

Kuvendi i Shqipërisë (Parliament of Albania) — unicameral legislature with 140 members elected by proportional representation. Citizens can contact their deputies through parlament.al. The Komisioni për Veprimtarinë Prodhuese (Committee on Production Activities) handles energy and Umwelt- legislation. Energy Community Treaty — Albania is a member of the Energy Community, which extends EU energy and Umwelt- acquis to Southeast European countries. Non-compliance can result in dispute settlement proceedings.

AM.s: Small landlocked Wirtschaft ($26B BIP) with significant Bergbau sector (min=6.79% of BIP, primarily copper-molybdenum at Kajaran and Sotk gold mine). Services-dominant (62% BIP). Geopolitical constraints from Nagorno-Karabakh conflict and closed borders with Turkey and Azerbaijan.

sectors.w: Armenia's Bergbau sector generates significant water pollution (Debed and Voghji rivers) and tailings dam risks. Wenn downstream water quality data is published alongside mine production revenue, W wird berechenbar from bilateral transactions.
bp: Mandate Umwelt- bonds and rehabilitation deposits that fully internalize mine closure costs. Wenn Bergbau companies must pre-fund Umwelt- remediation, das überlappende Interesse an extraction without cleanup wird geschwächt — marginal mines become unprofitable.

Mandatory real-time water quality monitoring downstream of all Bergbau operations, published openly. Armenia's EITI membership provides a disclosure framework — extending to Umwelt- monitoring data makes W visible from production data.

Teilweise brechbar. Open-pit Bergbau inherently disturbs landscapes and generates acid mine drainage. However, modern tailings management and water treatment can reduce the magnitude of Systemabhängigkeit.
a: Report Umwelt- violations at mine sites to the Environmental and Mining Inspection Body
Document water pollution downstream of tailings facilities — report to the Ministry of Environment
Use Armenia's Hinweisgeber protection provisions under the Anti-Corruption Strategy for reporting
Umsetzen international best practice tailings management (Global Industry Standard on Tailings Management)
Übernehmen EITI enhanced reporting including Umwelt- impact metrics
Commission independent water quality assessments for all operations
Ministry of Environment: Stärken Umwelt- impact assessment requirements for new Bergbau licenses
EMIB: Increase inspection frequency and enforce penalties for water pollution violations
Ministry of Territorial Administration: Require community consent processes for new mine approvals
Kontaktieren Sie Ihren member of the Azgayin Zhoghov (National Assembly) um zu fordern mandatory Umwelt- bonds for all Bergbau operations
Support strengthening of EITI reporting requirements to include Umwelt- impact data
Advocate for EU-Armenia CEPA (Comprehensive and Enhanced Partnership Agreement) enforcement on Umwelt- standards
Drängen auf international arbitration standards that include Umwelt- remediation in Bergbau investment disputes
i: Environmental and Mining Inspection Body (EMIB) — the Armenian government agency responsible for monitoring compliance with Umwelt- regulations at Bergbau operations, conducting inspections, and imposing Sanktionen. Ministry of Environment — oversees Umwelt- policy, issues Umwelt- impact assessment approvals, and sets water quality standards. Armenia's Anti-Corruption Council — established under the Prime Minister's office, provides channels for reporting corruption and misconduct in the Bergbau sector.

Zangezur Copper-Molybdenum Combine (ZCMC) — Armenia's largest Bergbau company, operating the Kajaran copper-molybdenum mine. Lydian International / Lydian Armenia — operator of the controversial Amulsar gold mine project. GeoProMining — Russian-owned company operating gold mines in Armenia. EITI — Armenia became an EITI candidate country in 2017, requiring disclosure of Bergbau revenues and contracts.

Ministry of Territorial Administration and Infrastructure — oversees Bergbau policy and licensing. Competition Protection Commission — monitors market concentration in the Bergbau sector. Armenian EITI Multi-Stakeholder Group — includes government, industry, and Zivilgesellschaft representatives overseeing extractive sector Transparenz.

Azgayin Zhoghov (National Assembly of Armenia) — unicameral parliament with 107 members. Citizens can contact their representatives through parliament.am. Armenia's 2018 revolution established stronger democratic institutions. EU-Armenia CEPA (Comprehensive and Enhanced Partnership Agreement) — signed in 2017, includes provisions on Umwelt- protection and sustainable development that Armenia has committed to implement.

AO.s: Major African oil producer with 29.28% combined oil and gas rents ($29.6B). Sonangol (state oil company) dominates the Wirtschaft. Agriculture (22.1% BIP) is largely subsistence. Post-civil war recovery continues with high inequality.

sectors.w: Angola's oil production is declining from mature deepwater fields. As extraction costs rise and global demand plateaus, the überlappendes Interesse between Sonangol and international partners (TotalEnergies, BP, ExxonMobil) wird geschwächt — declining reserves make diversification economically rational.
bp: Redirect Sonangol investment from deepwater exploration to erneuerbare Energie and agricultural modernization. Angola's solar potential is enormous (2,500+ kWh/m²/year). Wenn renewable returns exceed marginal barrel returns from declining fields, the überlappendes Interesse breaks.

Umsetzen EITI-standard revenue Transparenz extended to per-block Umwelt- impact data. Angola rejoined EITI in 2020 — extending disclosure to Emissionen and Umwelt- costs makes W computable per production unit.
Nicht brechbar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report Umwelt- violations at oil facilities to MIREMPET (Ministry of Mineral Resources, Petroleum and Gas)
Document community health impacts near production facilities through local NGOs
Report financial irregularities in Sonangol operations — Angola's anti-corruption framework (Lei 3/20) provides limited protections
Sonangol: Entwickeln strategic diversification plan with binding erneuerbare Energie investment targets
International partners (TotalEnergies, BP): Umsetzen Paris-aligned Emissionen reduction plans for Angolan operations
Übernehmen TCFD-aligned climate disclosure for all production sharing agreements
ANPG: Require Umwelt- impact assessments with community consultation for all new production blocks
Ministry of Environment: Stärken enforcement of Umwelt- regulations in the petroleum sector
Banco Nacional de Angola: Integrate climate risk into financial supervision of oil-dependent lending
Kontaktieren Sie Ihren member of the Assembleia Nacional um zu fordern publication of per-block Umwelt- impact data for all oil operations
Support EITI enhanced reporting with Umwelt- metrics for Angola
Eintreten über the African Union for continental erneuerbare Energie investment targets
Drängen auf Paris Agreement compliance monitoring through UNFCCC mechanisms
i: MIREMPET (Ministério dos Recursos Minerais, Petróleo e Gás) — Angola's ministry overseeing oil and gas operations, licensing, and regulation. ANPG (Agência Nacional de Petróleo, Gás e Biocombustíveis) — the national petroleum regulator, created in 2019 to replace Sonangol's dual role as regulator and operator. Lei 3/20 (Law on Public Probity) — Angola's anti-corruption law establishing asset declaration requirements and conflicts of interest rules for public officials. Whistleblower protection remains weak.

Sonangol E.P. — Angola's state oil company and the country's largest enterprise. Sonangol holds equity in all oil production sharing agreements and manages the state's petroleum interests. The company underwent restructuring in 2019, separating its regulatory functions to ANPG. TotalEnergies, BP, ExxonMobil, Eni — major international oil companies operating deepwater blocks offshore Angola.

ANPG (Agência Nacional de Petróleo, Gás e Biocombustíveis) — established in 2019 as the independent petroleum regulator, responsible for managing exploration and production contracts, monitoring compliance with license terms, and administering bidding rounds. Ministério do Ambiente — Angola's environment ministry, responsible for Umwelt- policy, impact assessments, and pollution control.

Assembleia Nacional (National Assembly of Angola) — unicameral legislature with 220 members. Angola's electoral system uses party-list proportional representation. The most recent elections were held in 2022. Citizens can engage through assembleia.ao. Angola is a presidential republic with significant executive power. EITI — Angola was suspended from EITI in 2017 for insufficient progress but successfully reapplied and was readmitted in 2020, committing to enhanced Transparenz standards.

AR.s: G20 Wirtschaft ($638.4B BIP) with massive Vaca Muerta shale formation (second-largest shale gas, fourth-largest shale oil reserves globally). Agricultural powerhouse (soy, beef) with 5.8% ag BIP share but outsized Umwelt- footprint. Chronic macroeconomic instability.

sectors.w: Argentina's Vaca Muerta development requires massive capital investment ($10B+/year) and government subsidies (Plan Gas). Removing subsidies breaks the überlappendes Interesse — without price guarantees, many Vaca Muerta wells are marginal at global market prices.
bp: Phase out Plan Gas subsidies and redirect capital to erneuerbare Energie. Argentina has world-class wind resources in Patagonia and solar in the northwest. Wenn renewable electricity undercuts subsidized gas, the überlappendes Interesse between producers and government in continued fossil expansion breaks.

Mandatory per-well Emissionen disclosure including methane venting and flaring. Argentina's Secretaría de Energía already collects production data — extending to Emissionen makes W computable from production statistics.
Nicht brechbar by policy alone. Shale gas extraction and combustion release CO₂ and methane by physical process.
a: Report Umwelt- violations at Vaca Muerta operations to the provincial Umweltbehörde (Secretaría de Ambiente de Neuquén)
Document water contamination from fracking operations — report to AIC (Autoridad Interjurisdiccional de las Cuencas)
Report safety violations to the Secretaría de Trabajo
YPF: Entwickeln Paris-aligned transition plan with binding Emissionen reduction targets for Vaca Muerta operations
Umsetzen TCFD-aligned climate disclosure across all operations
Investieren in CCUS (carbon capture, utilization, and storage) pilot projects for gas processing facilities
Secretaría de Energía: Publish per-well Emissionen data for all Vaca Muerta operations
SAyDS: Stärken federal Umwelt- standards for unconventional hydrocarbon extraction
ENARGAS: Reformieren natural gas tariff structure to eliminate implicit fossil fuel subsidies
Kontaktieren Sie Ihren Diputado Nacional or Senador um zu fordern a timeline for fossil fuel subsidy phase-out and transparent per-well Emissionen reporting
Stärken Argentina's NDC — current targets are considered insufficient by Climate Action Tracker
Leverage Mercosur-EU trade agreement Umwelt- provisions to align energy transition commitments
Support South American erneuerbare Energie integration through regional grid interconnection
i: Secretaría de Ambiente de Neuquén — Neuquén province's Umweltbehörde, responsible for Umwelt- permitting and monitoring of oil and gas operations in the Vaca Muerta formation. AIC (Autoridad Interjurisdiccional de las Cuencas de los ríos Limay, Neuquén y Negro) — the inter-jurisdictional water authority managing the river basins affected by Vaca Muerta operations. COFEMA (Consejo Federal de Medio Ambiente) — federal-provincial Umwelt- coordination body. Argentina lacks comprehensive federal Hinweisgeber protection legislation, though provincial laws vary.

YPF S.A. — Argentina's largest energy company (51% staatliches), the dominant operator in Vaca Muerta. YPF's strategic decisions on Vaca Muerta development drive national energy policy. Pan American Energy, Vista Energy, TotalEnergies — other major operators in Vaca Muerta. Plan Gas.Ar — the Argentine government's gas production incentive program, guaranteeing minimum prices for domestic gas production to stimulate Vaca Muerta development.

Secretaría de Energía — Argentina's energy secretariat, responsible for energy policy, production monitoring, and regulatory oversight. SAyDS (Secretaría de Ambiente y Desarrollo Sustentable) — the federal environment secretariat, responsible for Umwelt- policy and Klimawandel. ENARGAS (Ente Nacional Regulador del Gas) — the national gas regulatory authority, overseeing gas transport, distribution, and tariff regulation.

Congreso de la Nación Argentina — bicameral legislature consisting of the Cámara de Diputados (257 members) and the Senado (72 members). Citizens can contact representatives through diputados.gov.ar and senado.gob.ar. The Comisión de Energía y Combustibles handles energy legislation. Argentina's NDC — Argentina submitted an updated NDC in 2021 targeting Emissionen of no more than 349 MtCO₂e by 2030. Climate Action Tracker rates this as 'highly insufficient.'

AT.s: Wealthy EU Wirtschaft ($534.8B BIP) with strong manufacturing (15.1% of BIP). Low direct fossil fuel exposure but significant UPF and financial sector PST. Major transit hub for Russian gas (Baumgarten hub). High healthcare expenditure (11.83% BIP) suggests diet-related disease burden.

sectors.w: Austria's healthcare system (ASVG social insurance) tracks diet-related disease costs with high granularity. Wenn per-product-category healthcare costs are published alongside retail revenue, W wird berechenbar from bilateral UPF transactions.
bp: Umsetzen front-of-pack health-impact labelling through EU Farm to Fork Strategy. Wenn consumers see welfare costs at point of sale, the überlappendes Interesse between UPF manufacturers and retailers in high-margin processed products wird geschwächt.

Mandate publication of diet-attributable healthcare costs per product category using ASVG social insurance data. Austria's comprehensive health insurance system generates the data needed to make W computable.
Not fully breakable. Ultra-processing alters food matrix in ways that affect metabolic response. Reformierenulation mandates can reduce but not eliminate Systemabhängigkeit.
a: Report misleading health claims on food products to the Bundeswettbewerbsbehörde (Federal Competition Authority)
Document undisclosed processing methods — report to AGES (Austrian Agency for Health and Food Safety)
Use the HinweisgeberInnenschutzgesetz (Whistleblower Protection Act, 2023) for protected reporting
Reformierenulate product lines to reduce ultra-processing — invest in minimally processed alternatives
Voluntarily adopt front-of-pack nutritional labelling ahead of EU mandate
Commission health-impact assessment of product portfolio using national health insurance data
BMSGPK: Umsetzen mandatory front-of-pack health labelling for UPF products
AGES: Publish annual diet-attributable disease burden data linked to specific product categories
Hauptverband der Sozialversicherungsträger: Release anonymized diet-related healthcare cost data
Kontaktieren Sie Ihren member of the Nationalrat or Bundesrat um zu fordern mandatory health-impact labelling on ultra-processed foods
Support EU-wide adoption of front-of-pack nutritional labelling
Advocate for inclusion of diet-attributable health costs in national accounting
Drängen auf WHO-aligned UPF marketing restrictions through EU legislative process
i: Bundeswettbewerbsbehörde (BWB / Federal Competition Authority) — Austria's competition authority, investigating unfair commercial practices including misleading advertising. AGES (Agentur für Gesundheit und Ernährungssicherheit / Austrian Agency for Health and Food Safety) — the federal agency responsible for food safety, nutrition surveillance, and public health. AGES conducts food inspections and risk assessments. HinweisgeberInnenschutzgesetz (HSchG, 2023) — Austria's transposition of the EU Whistleblower Directive, providing protection for employees reporting violations of EU or national law.

EU Farm to Fork Strategy — includes proposals for mandatory front-of-pack nutrition labelling across the EU. Austrian food companies that move early gain competitive advantage in the EU single market. EFSA (European Food Safety Authority) — provides scientific opinions that shape EU food regulation applicable in Austria.

BMSGPK (Bundesministerium für Soziales, Gesundheit, Pflege und Konsumentenschutz / Federal Ministry of Social Affairs, Health, Care and Consumer Protection) — oversees food safety policy, nutrition guidelines, and consumer protection. Hauptverband der österreichischen Sozialversicherungsträger (now Dachverband) — the umbrella organization of Austrian social insurance institutions, managing healthcare data for the entire insured population.

Österreichisches Parlament — Austria's bicameral parliament consisting of the Nationalrat (183 members) and the Bundesrat (61 members). Citizens can contact representatives through parlament.gv.at. The Gesundheitsausschuss (Health Committee) handles health and food policy legislation.

AZ.s: Oil-dependent Wirtschaft with 29.57% combined oil and gas rents ($22B). SOCAR (State Oil Company) dominates. Hosted COP29 (2024). Authoritarian governance with limited Zivilgesellschaft space. Caspian Sea oil and gas fields (ACG, Shah Deniz) are mature.

sectors.w: Azerbaijan's oil production is declining from mature Caspian fields (ACG peaked in 2010). As production costs rise and reserves deplete, the überlappendes Interesse between SOCAR and international partners (BP) wird geschwächt — diversification becomes economically necessary.
bp: Accelerate post-oil diversification using SOFAZ (State Oil Fund) reserves. Azerbaijan's $50B+ sovereign wealth fund can finance erneuerbare Energie and non-oil economic development. Wenn SOFAZ investment returns from renewables exceed declining oil returns, the überlappendes Interesse breaks.
Umsetzen pro Feld Emissionen disclosure for all Caspian operations. BP already publishes Emissionen data for ACG — extending to all SOCAR operations makes W computable across the sector.
Nicht brechbar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report Umwelt- violations at oil facilities to the Ministry of Ecology and Natural Resources
Document Emissionen underreporting through internal SOCAR channels
Report Umwelt- concerns to international partners (BP) through their compliance systems
SOCAR: Entwickeln binding erneuerbare Energie transition plan with interim Emissionen targets
BP Azerbaijan: Extend ACG Emissionen Transparenz standards to all partner operations
SOFAZ: Übernehmen ESG investment criteria excluding new fossil fuel development
Ministry of Ecology: Stärken Umwelt- standards for offshore Caspian operations
EITI Azerbaijan: Expand reporting to include Umwelt- impact data per production block
Tariff Council: Reformieren energy pricing to remove implicit fossil fuel consumption subsidies
Leverage Azerbaijan's COP29 presidency legacy to strengthen national climate commitments
Eintreten über the Tehran Convention for Caspian Sea Umwelt- protection standards
Support EITI enhanced reporting with Umwelt- metrics
Drängen auf European Energy Charter Treaty enforcement on Umwelt- standards in transit countries
i: Ministry of Ecology and Natural Resources — Azerbaijan's Umweltbehörde, responsible for Umwelt- monitoring, permitting, and enforcement. The Ministry oversees Umwelt- impact assessments for energy projects. SOCAR (State Oil Company of the Azerbaijan Republic) — the national oil company controlling all upstream and downstream petroleum operations. Note: Azerbaijan has no comprehensive Hinweisgeber protection law. Civil society space is severely constrained; independent media and NGOs face significant operational barriers.

SOCAR — Azerbaijan's state oil company, operating the country's refining, petrochemical, and upstream assets. SOCAR also has international operations in Turkey, Georgia, and Europe. BP — the largest international operator in Azerbaijan, managing the Azeri-Chirag-Deepwater Gunashli (ACG) oilfield and Shah Deniz gas field through Production Sharing Agreements. SOFAZ (State Oil Fund of the Republic of Azerbaijan) — the sovereign wealth fund managing Azerbaijan's petroleum revenues, with assets exceeding $50B.

Azerbaijan EITI — Azerbaijan was an early EITI member (2003) and publishes annual reports on extractive industry revenues. The EITI process provides one of the few independent Transparenz mechanisms in the sector. Tariff (Price) Council — sets regulated energy prices in Azerbaijan. Tehran Convention — the Framework Convention for the Protection of the Marine Environment of the Caspian Sea, which governs Umwelt- standards for offshore operations.

Milli Majlis (National Assembly of Azerbaijan) — unicameral parliament with 125 members. Note: Azerbaijan is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House; elections are not considered free and fair by international observers. Civic engagement channels are limited. International pressure operates primarily through multilateral forums including the Council of Europe (Azerbaijan is a member), OSCE, and the EITI Board. The EU-Azerbaijan Partnership and Cooperation Agreement provides a framework for dialogue on energy and Umwelt- issues.

BA.s: Post-conflict Wirtschaft ($29.6B BIP) with complex governance (two entities: Federation and Republika Srpska). Coal-dependent energy sector and manufacturing (12.7% BIP). EU-Kandidat country. Significant coal Bergbau and thermal power generation.

sectors.w: Bosnia's coal sector is heavily subsidized through below-market electricity pricing and state employment guarantees. EU accession requires alignment with the EU ETS — when carbon pricing applies, the überlappendes Interesse between coal producers and the state employment system breaks.
bp: EU accession alignment with EU ETS carbon pricing. Wenn Bosnian coal power must pay the carbon price (~€90/ton), coal-fired electricity becomes uncompetitive with imported renewables. The überlappendes Interesse between mine operators and state employment programs breaks.

Mandatory Emissionen monitoring and disclosure per power plant. Energy Community Treaty membership requires EU-aligned Umwelt- reporting — implementing this makes W computable from electricity generation data.
Nicht brechbar. Coal combustion releases CO₂, SO₂, and particulate matter by physical process.
a: Report Umwelt- violations at coal mines and power plants to entity-level Umwelt- inspectorates
Document health impacts in communities near coal power plants — report to public health institutes
Report corruption in coal procurement to the Agency for Prevention of Corruption and Coordination of the Fight Against Corruption
State electricity companies (EPBiH, ERS): Entwickeln coal phase-out timelines aligned with Energy Community Treaty obligations
Investieren in erneuerbare Energie capacity to replace aging coal plants
Umsetzen Emissionen monitoring per facility ahead of EU ETS alignment
DERK (State Electricity Regulatory Commission): Reformieren tariffs to reflect true Umwelt- costs of coal generation
Entity Umwelt- agencies: Durchsetzen air quality standards at coal power plants
Energy Community Secretariat: Use dispute resolution to enforce Umwelt- compliance
Kontaktieren Sie Ihren member of the Parlamentarna skupština um zu fordern coal phase-out timelines aligned with Energy Community obligations
Support EU accession negotiations contingent on energy transition commitments
Advocate for just transition funding for coal-dependent communities through IPA III (EU Instrument for Pre-Accession)
Drängen auf Energy Community Treaty enforcement on air quality standards
i: Environmental inspection is split between the Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina and Republika Srpska, each maintaining separate Umwelt- agencies. Federation: Federalni inspektorat — Umwelt- inspectorate. Republika Srpska: Republička uprava za inspekcijske poslove — inspection administration. Agency for Prevention of Corruption and Coordination of the Fight Against Corruption (APIK) — state-level anti-corruption body. Bosnia lacks comprehensive Hinweisgeber protection legislation, though draft laws have been discussed as part of EU accession reforms.

EPBiH (Elektroprivreda Bosne i Hercegovine) — the Federation's state electricity company, operating coal-fired power plants and hydropower. ERS (Elektroprivreda Republike Srpske) — Republika Srpska's state electricity company. Both companies operate aging coal infrastructure (many plants dating to Yugoslav era) and are major employers in Bergbau communities.

DERK (Državna regulatorna komisija za električnu energiju / State Electricity Regulatory Commission) — the state-level electricity regulator. FERK and RERS are entity-level regulators. Energy Community Treaty — Bosnia is a contracting party, committed to implementing EU energy and Umwelt- acquis including Large Combustion Plants Directive and Industrial Emissions Directive.

Parlamentarna skupština Bosne i Hercegovine (Parliamentary Assembly) — bicameral legislature consisting of the Predstavnički dom (House of Representatives, 42 members) and the Dom naroda (House of Peoples, 15 members). Bosnia's complex governance structure (Dayton Agreement) divides power between state, entity, and cantonal levels. Citizens can contact representatives through parlament.ba.

BD.s: Large Wirtschaft ($450.1B BIP) driven by garment manufacturing (21.9% mfg share of BIP). High agricultural employment (11.2% BIP). Extreme climate vulnerability — low-lying delta at severe flood and cyclone risk. One of the world's most densely populated countries.

sectors.w: Bangladesh's rice monoculture depletes groundwater (arsenic contamination) and reduces soil biodiversity. Satellite-based crop monitoring and water quality data from BWDB can make W computable from agricultural output and water extraction volumes.
bp: Reformieren agricultural subsidies from input-based (fertilizer, diesel) to outcome-based (soil health, water efficiency). Wenn subsidies reward diversification rather than rice intensification, das überlappende Interesse an monoculture between farmers and input suppliers wird geschwächt.

Mandatory groundwater extraction monitoring and arsenic contamination mapping published at upazila level. BWDB already collects water data — extending to public disclosure linked to agricultural output makes W computable.

Teilweise brechbar. Intensive rice cultivation in deltaic conditions inherently alters hydrology and nutrient cycling. However, System of Rice Intensification (SRI) and crop diversification can reduce Systemabhängigkeit.
a: Report pesticide misuse or adulteration to DAE (Department of Agricultural Extension)
Document groundwater over-extraction or arsenic contamination — report to DPHE (Department of Public Health Engineering)
Report food safety violations in agricultural supply chains to BFSA (Bangladesh Food Safety Authority)
Agricultural input companies: Entwickeln integrated pest management products as alternatives to chemical-intensive packages
Investieren in climate-resilient crop varieties through partnerships with BRRI (Bangladesh Rice Research Institute)
Übernehmen water-use efficiency targets across supply chains
Ministry of Agriculture: Reformieren subsidy structure from input-based to outcome-based
BWDB: Umsetzen mandatory groundwater monitoring networks in intensive agricultural zones
DoE (Department of Environment): Durchsetzen regulations on agricultural chemical runoff into waterways
Kontaktieren Sie Ihren member of the Jatiya Sangsad um zu fordern reform of agricultural subsidies toward climate resilience and crop diversification
Support Bangladesh's Climate Vulnerable Forum leadership in advocating for agricultural adaptation finance
Advocate for inclusion of groundwater depletion costs in national BIP accounting
Drängen auf international climate finance to support agricultural diversification away from rice monoculture
i: DAE (Department of Agricultural Extension) — the primary government agency for agricultural technical support and extension services, operating at union, upazila, and district levels. DPHE (Department of Public Health Engineering) — responsible for water supply and sanitation, including monitoring arsenic contamination in groundwater (affecting ~35 million people). BFSA (Bangladesh Food Safety Authority) — established in 2015 to regulate food safety across the supply chain. Bangladesh lacks comprehensive Hinweisgeber protection legislation.

BRRI (Bangladesh Rice Research Institute) — the national research institute developing improved rice varieties, including flood-tolerant (BRRI dhan52), salt-tolerant, and drought-resistant varieties. BARI (Bangladesh Agricultural Research Institute) — conducts research on non-rice crops including vegetables, pulses, and oilseeds. BADC (Bangladesh Agricultural Entwickelnment Corporation) — the government agency responsible for seed production and distribution.

Ministry of Agriculture — oversees agricultural policy, subsidies, and development programs. BWDB (Bangladesh Water Entwickelnment Board) — responsible for flood control, drainage, and irrigation infrastructure. The BWDB manages the country's water resource data system. DoE (Department of Environment) — Bangladesh's Umwelt- regulatory agency, responsible for Umwelt- clearance of projects and monitoring pollution.

Jatiya Sangsad (National Parliament of Bangladesh) — unicameral legislature with 350 members (300 directly elected, 50 reserved for women). Citizens can contact representatives through parliament.gov.bd. The Standing Committee on Ministry of Agriculture handles agricultural policy. Bangladesh Climate Change Strategy and Action Plan (BCCSAP) — the national framework for climate adaptation and mitigation, including agricultural resilience.

BE.s: EU and NATO headquarters Wirtschaft ($671.4B BIP). Services-dominant (71% BIP). Major port (Antwerp — Europe's second-largest) handles significant fossil fuel and petrochemical throughput. High healthcare expenditure (10.8% BIP). Federal state with three regions.

sectors.w: Belgium's comprehensive INAMI/RIZIV health insurance system tracks healthcare costs with high granularity. Publishing diet-attributable disease costs per product category makes W computable from retail sales data.
bp: Umsetzen front-of-pack health labelling (Belgium adopted Nutri-Score voluntarily in 2019). Extending to mandatory application and adding health-cost information wird geschwächt das überlappende Interesse an high-margin UPF.
INAMI/RIZIV publication of anonymized diet-attributable healthcare cost data per product category. Belgium's universal health insurance generates comprehensive data to make W computable.
Not fully breakable. Ultra-processing alters food matrix affecting metabolic response. Reformierenulation mandates reduce but do not eliminate Systemabhängigkeit.
a: Report misleading health claims to SPF Économie (Federal Public Service Economy)
Document food safety violations — report to AFSCA/FAVV (Federal Agency for the Safety of the Food Chain)
Use the Belgian Hinweisgeber protection law (transposition of EU Directive 2019/1937) for protected reporting
Reformierenulate product lines to reduce ultra-processing
Extend voluntary Nutri-Score adoption to all product lines
Commission health-impact assessment of product portfolio
SPF Santé publique: Make Nutri-Score mandatory for all packaged food products
AFSCA/FAVV: Stärken enforcement of marketing claims on UPF products
INAMI/RIZIV: Publish annual reports linking food consumption patterns to healthcare expenditure
Kontaktieren Sie Ihren member of the Chambre des représentants / Kamer van Volksvertegenwoordigers um zu fordern mandatory health-impact labelling
Support EU-wide mandatory Nutri-Score through the Farm to Fork Strategy
Advocate for WHO-aligned UPF marketing restrictions
Drängen auf inclusion of diet-related health costs in national accounting frameworks
i: AFSCA/FAVV (Agence fédérale pour la sécurité de la chaîne alimentaire / Federaal Agentschap voor de Veiligheid van de Voedselketen) — Belgium's federal food safety agency, responsible for food chain safety from farm to fork. Conducts inspections, sampling, and enforcement. SPF Économie (Service public fédéral Économie) — the federal economic affairs service handling consumer protection and fair commercial practices.

Fevia (Federation of the Belgian Food Industry) — the trade association representing over 700 food companies in Belgium. Fevia's sustainability commitments influence industry practices. Belgium hosts major food companies including Puratos, Vandemoortele, and Lotus Bakeries.

SPF Santé publique (Federal Public Service Health, Food Chain Safety and Environment) — the federal ministry overseeing public health policy, food safety, and environment. INAMI/RIZIV (Institut national d'assurance maladie-invalidité / Rijksinstituut voor ziekte- en invaliditeitsverzekering) — Belgium's national health insurance institute, managing the compulsory health insurance system and healthcare cost data.

Parlement fédéral / Federaal Parlement — Belgium's bicameral parliament consisting of the Chambre des représentants / Kamer van Volksvertegenwoordigers (150 members) and the Sénat / Senaat (60 members). Citizens can contact representatives through lachambre.be / dekamer.be. Belgium's federal structure means health policy is shared between federal and regional (community) governments.

BF.s: Low-income Sahelian Wirtschaft ($23.1B BIP) with dominant gold Bergbau sector (min=15.45% BIP). Agriculture employs 80%+ of population (20.9% BIP). Military junta since 2022 coup. Severe security challenges (jihadist insurgency).

sectors.w: Burkina Faso's gold Bergbau generates cyanide contamination, mercury pollution (artisanal Bergbau), and community displacement. Wenn Umwelt- and health costs per mine are published alongside production revenue, W wird berechenbar.
bp: Mandate Umwelt- bonds and community benefit agreements for all industrial Bergbau licenses. Wenn Bergbau companies must pre-fund Umwelt- remediation and community compensation, das überlappende Interesse an extraction without accountability wird geschwächt.

Publish pro Mine Umwelt- impact data (water quality, air quality, land disturbance) alongside production revenue through EITI reporting. Burkina Faso's EITI membership provides the framework — extending to Umwelt- data makes W visible.

Teilweise brechbar. Open-pit gold Bergbau inherently disturbs landscapes and generates toxic waste. However, improved tailings management and mercury-free artisanal methods can reduce Systemabhängigkeit.
a: Report Umwelt- violations at mine sites to BUNEE (Bureau National des Évaluations Environnementales)
Document community health impacts near Bergbau operations through local health clinics
Report illegal artisanal Bergbau activities and mercury use to provincial authorities
Mining companies (Endeavour Mining, IAMGOLD, Nordgold): Umsetzen Global Industry Standard on Tailings Management
Übernehmen EITI enhanced reporting including Umwelt- impact metrics
Investieren in mercury-free processing technologies for artisanal Bergbau support programs
Ministry of Mines: Stärken Umwelt- conditions in Bergbau permits and enforce compliance
BUMIGEB (Bureau des Mines et de la Géologie): Improve artisanal Bergbau regulation to reduce mercury use
EITI-BF: Expand reporting to include Umwelt- and community impact data
Eintreten über ECOWAS for regional Bergbau Umwelt- standards
Support African Mining Vision implementation for sustainable resource governance
Drängen auf international Bergbau companies to adopt binding Umwelt- standards through OECD Due Diligence Guidance
Support Kimberley Process-style certification for gold supply chains to reduce conflict mineral financing
i: BUNEE (Bureau National des Évaluations Environnementales) — Burkina Faso's national Umwelt- assessment authority, responsible for Umwelt- impact assessments of Bergbau projects. Ministère de l'Environnement, de l'Énergie, de l'Eau et de l'Assainissement — oversees Umwelt- policy and regulation. Note: Under military rule since 2022, institutional capacity is constrained and Zivilgesellschaft space is restricted.

Major Bergbau companies operating in Burkina Faso include Endeavour Mining (Houndé, Wahgnion mines), IAMGOLD (Essakane mine), and Nordgold (Bissa-Bouly). These international companies are subject to home-country ESG reporting requirements and international Bergbau standards (ICMM, IFC Performance Standards).

Ministère des Mines et des Carrières — the ministry responsible for Bergbau policy, licensing, and regulation. BUMIGEB (Bureau des Mines et de la Géologie du Burkina) — the geological survey and Bergbau technical agency. EITI Burkina Faso — Burkina Faso has been an EITI member since 2009, publishing reports on Bergbau revenues. The 2015 Mining Code establishes Umwelt- obligations for Bergbau companies.

Note: Burkina Faso is under military rule (Capitaine Ibrahim Traoré since 2022). The Assemblée Législative de Transition (Transitional Legislative Assembly) replaced the elected National Assembly. Democratic institutions are suspended. International engagement channels: ECOWAS (Economic Community of West African States — though Burkina Faso announced withdrawal in 2024), African Union, and international development partners. The African Mining Vision (2009) provides a continental framework for sustainable Bergbau governance.

BG.s: EU-Mitglied Wirtschaft ($113.3B BIP) with legacy coal dependence (Maritsa East complex is one of Europe's largest lignite basins). Services-dominant (63.8% BIP). EU accession has driven institutional modernization but coal transition remains politically sensitive.

sectors.w: Bulgaria's coal sector is sustained by implicit subsidies (below-market electricity pricing, state employment). EU ETS carbon pricing makes lignite uncompetitive — when full carbon costs are internalized, the überlappendes Interesse between mine operators and state employment programs breaks.
bp: Full alignment with EU ETS free allowance phase-out. Wenn Bulgarian lignite plants pay the EU carbon price without free allowances, coal-fired electricity becomes uncompetitive. The überlappendes Interesse breaks when renewable alternatives are cheaper.

Mandatory pro Anlage Emissionen and health-impact disclosure. Bulgaria's lignite plants (Maritsa East I, II, III) are among Europe's worst air polluters. Publishing health costs per MWh makes W computable from electricity generation data.
Nicht brechbar. Lignite combustion releases CO₂, SO₂, NOx, and particulate matter by physical process.
a: Report Emissionen violations at coal plants to RIOSV (Regional Inspectorates of Environment and Water)
Document health impacts in communities near Maritsa East complex — report to RZI (Regional Health Inspectorates)
Use Bulgaria's Hinweisgeber protection law (transposition of EU Directive 2019/1937) for protected reporting
Maritsa East complex operators: Entwickeln coal phase-out plans with worker transition programs
Investieren in erneuerbare Energie capacity (Bulgaria has strong solar potential — 1,500+ kWh/m²/year)
Übernehmen per-unit Emissionen reporting aligned with EU taxonomy
KEVR (Energy and Water Regulatory Commission): Reformieren electricity tariffs to reflect carbon costs
Ministry of Energy: Entwickeln binding coal phase-out timeline aligned with EU Green Deal
Ministry of Environment: Durchsetzen Industrial Emissions Directive compliance at all coal plants
Kontaktieren Sie Ihren member of the Narodno Sabranie (National Assembly) um zu fordern a binding coal phase-out timeline with just transition funding
Support EU Just Transition Fund allocation for Stara Zagora and other coal-dependent regions
Advocate for accelerated EU ETS free allowance phase-out
Drängen auf Territorial Just Transition Plans that include concrete retraining and economic diversification measures
i: RIOSV (Регионални инспекции по околната среда и водите / Regional Inspectorates of Environment and Water) — 16 regional Umwelt- agencies conducting inspections and enforcement.
