EEA (Изпълнителна агенция по околна среда / Executive Environment Agency) — the national Umwelt- monitoring agency collecting Emissionen and air quality data. Bulgaria transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2023.

Maritsa East complex — comprises three lignite-fired power plants (Maritsa East I, II, III) and the associated Maritsa-Iztok mines. AES Bulgaria operates Maritsa East I; ContourGlobal operates Maritsa East III. The complex is the largest industrial employer in the Stara Zagora region. Mini Maritsa-Iztok — the staatliches lignite Bergbau company supplying the Maritsa East plants.

KEVR (Комисия за енергийно и водно регулиране / Energy and Water Regulatory Commission) — Bulgaria's independent Energieregulierungsbehörde, setting electricity tariffs and licensing. Ministry of Energy — oversees energy policy including the National Energy and Climate Plan (NECP). Bulgaria's NECP has been criticized for insufficient coal phase-out ambition.

Narodno Sabranie (Народно събрание / National Assembly of Bulgaria) — unicameral parliament with 240 members. Citizens can contact representatives through parliament.bg. The Комисия по енергетика (Energy Committee) handles energy legislation. EU Just Transition Fund — Bulgaria is eligible for significant JTF funding to support coal transition in affected regions. Bulgaria's Territorial Just Transition Plans cover Stara Zagora, Kyustendil, and Pernik regions.

BH.s: Small island petrostate ($47.1B BIP) with 16.64% combined oil and gas rents. Bahrain's Abu Safa offshore field (shared with Saudi Arabia) provides the majority of oil revenue. Diversifying into financial services (Bahrain Financial Harbour) and aluminum (Alba smelter).

sectors.w: Bahrain's oil reserves are the smallest among GCC states (declining production from mature fields). The economic case for diversification is strongest here — when post-oil planning makes renewable investment more attractive than marginal barrel extraction, the überlappendes Interesse breaks.
bp: Accelerate Economic Vision 2030 diversification away from oil dependence. Bahrain's small size and advanced financial sector make it the most transition-ready GCC Wirtschaft. Wenn financial services and technology revenue exceeds oil revenue, das überlappende Interesse an extraction breaks.
Publish pro Feld Umwelt- and Emissionen data for all Bahrain Petroleum Company (BAPCO) operations. Making lifecycle Emissionen visible per barrel makes W computable from production data.
Nicht brechbar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report Umwelt- violations at BAPCO facilities to the Supreme Council for Environment
Document Emissionen or safety concerns through internal BAPCO compliance channels
Report concerns to Bahrain's National Audit Office regarding mismanagement of petroleum revenues
BAPCO: Accelerate the BAPCO Modernization Program to include erneuerbare Energie components
Mumtalakat (sovereign wealth fund): Increase erneuerbare Energie allocation in investment portfolio
Umsetzen TCFD-aligned climate disclosure for all operations
NOGA: Require Emissionen disclosure for all petroleum operations
SCE: Stärken Umwelt- standards for refinery operations
CBB (Central Bank of Bahrain): Integrate climate risk disclosure into financial sector regulation
Eintreten über GCC Secretariat for regional carbon pricing mechanisms
Support IRENA recommendations on GCC energy transition pathways
Drängen auf enhanced climate disclosure standards through the Bahrain Bourse ESG framework
Leverage Bahrain's UNFCCC commitments to strengthen national Emissionen reduction targets
i: Supreme Council for Environment (SCE) — Bahrain's Umweltbehörde, responsible for Umwelt- policy, permitting, and enforcement. SCE oversees Umwelt- impact assessments for industrial projects. BAPCO (Bahrain Petroleum Company) — the staatliches oil company operating Bahrain's refinery and upstream assets. National Audit Office — conducts financial audits of government entities including BAPCO. Note: Bahrain has limited Hinweisgeber protection; Zivilgesellschaft space is constrained.

BAPCO (Bahrain Petroleum Company) — operates the Sitra refinery (267,000 bpd after modernization) and manages upstream production. Mumtalakat — Bahrain's sovereign wealth fund, managing state assets including BAPCO and Alba (Aluminium Bahrain). Nogaholding — the investment arm of Bahrain's National Oil and Gas Authority, overseeing the country's energy sector investments.

NOGA (National Oil and Gas Authority) — the government body responsible for oil and gas policy, licensing, and upstream regulation. CBB (Central Bank of Bahrain) — the financial sector regulator, which has begun integrating ESG considerations into banking supervision. Bahrain Economic Entwickelnment Board — coordinates the Economic Vision 2030 diversification strategy.

Council of Representatives (Majlis al-Nuwab) — the elected lower house of Bahrain's parliament (40 members) and the Shura Council (Majlis al-Shura, 40 appointed members). Note: Bahrain is a konstitutionelle Monarchie with significant royal authority. Political opposition is restricted. The Council of Representatives has limited legislative power. International engagement on climate issues operates through GCC coordination and UNFCCC processes.

BJ.s: Small West African Wirtschaft ($21.5B BIP) with dominant agriculture sector (24.2% BIP, primarily cotton). Services growing (48.9% BIP). Cotton is the main export crop — Benin is Africa's largest cotton producer. Climate vulnerability through Sahel desertification and coastal erosion.

sectors.w: Benin's cotton monoculture degrades soil fertility and increases pesticide exposure. Wenn soil health data and pesticide-related healthcare costs are published alongside cotton production revenue, W wird berechenbar from agricultural output data.
bp: Reformieren cotton subsidies to reward soil health and crop diversification. Wenn input subsidies (seeds, fertilizer, pesticides) are redirected from cotton intensification to mixed farming systems, the überlappendes Interesse between cotton traders and input suppliers wird geschwächt.

Mandatory publication of soil health metrics (organic matter, nutrient balance) and pesticide-related health costs per cotton-producing commune. Making Umwelt- and health costs visible makes W computable.

Teilweise brechbar. Cotton monoculture inherently depletes soil nutrients and requires pesticide application. However, integrated pest management and organic cotton production can reduce Systemabhängigkeit — Benin has a growing organic cotton sector.
a: Report pesticide misuse or adulteration to DPQC (Direction de la Promotion de la Qualité et du Conditionnement des Produits Agricoles)
Document health impacts of pesticide exposure in cotton-growing communities — report to local health centers
Report corruption in cotton sector management to the Front des Organisations Nationales contre la Corruption (FONAC)
AIC (Association Interprofessionnelle du Coton): Promote integrated pest management to reduce chemical dependency
Investieren in organic cotton certification and premium markets
Übernehmen soil health monitoring across cotton supply chains
MAEP (Ministry of Agriculture): Reformieren cotton input subsidy programs to incentivize sustainable practices
ABE: Stärken Umwelt- impact monitoring in cotton-growing zones
INSAE (National Statistics Institute): Include soil health and agricultural Umwelt- indicators in national surveys
Kontaktieren Sie Ihren Député in the Assemblée Nationale um zu fordern reform of cotton input subsidies toward sustainable agriculture
Eintreten über ECOWAS for regional agricultural sustainability standards
Drängen auf Cotton-4 (Benin, Burkina Faso, Chad, Mali) coordination on sustainable cotton production
Support WTO negotiations on cotton subsidy reform to level the playing field for African producers
i: DPQC (Direction de la Promotion de la Qualité et du Conditionnement des Produits Agricoles) — the government directorate responsible for agricultural product quality and standards. ABE (Agence Béninoise pour l'Environnement) — Benin's Umwelt- agency, responsible for Umwelt- impact assessment and monitoring. FONAC (Front des Organisations Nationales contre la Corruption) — a national anti-corruption coalition of Zivilgesellschaft organizations.

AIC (Association Interprofessionnelle du Coton) — the inter-professional cotton association coordinating relations between producers, ginners, and input suppliers. SODECO (Société de Développement du Coton) — the main cotton ginning company. The cotton sector was partially liberalized but remains state-coordinated through the AIC framework.

MAEP (Ministère de l'Agriculture, de l'Élevage et de la Pêche) — Benin's agriculture ministry, responsible for agricultural policy and cotton sector coordination. ABE (Agence Béninoise pour l'Environnement) — conducts Umwelt- monitoring and impact assessments. INSAE (Institut National de la Statistique et de l'Analyse Économique) — Benin's national statistics institute.

Assemblée Nationale du Bénin — unicameral parliament with 109 members. Citizens can contact their deputies through the official Assembly website. Note: Benin's democratic space has narrowed under recent constitutional reforms, though elections continue. Cotton-4 group — Benin, Burkina Faso, Chad, and Mali coordinate on cotton trade policy at the WTO, advocating against subsidies in developed countries that depress global cotton prices.

BN.s: Small wealthy petrostate ($15.3B BIP) with 24.23% combined oil and gas rents. Brunei Shell Petroleum (BSP, 50/50 JV with Shell) dominates the Wirtschaft. Absolute monarchy. Wawasan Brunei 2035 diversification strategy underway but oil/gas remain 60%+ of government revenue.

sectors.w: Brunei's oil production is in long-term decline (from 220,000 bpd peak to ~100,000 bpd). As reserves deplete, the überlappendes Interesse between BSP and the government in continued extraction wird geschwächt — Wawasan 2035 diversification becomes economically necessary.
bp: Accelerate Wawasan 2035 economic diversification using Brunei Investment Agency (BIA) sovereign wealth. Wenn non-oil sectors generate more government revenue than declining oil production, das überlappende Interesse an extraction breaks.
Umsetzen pro Feld Emissionen disclosure for all BSP and Total operations. Shell's global ESG reporting standards can be extended to Brunei operations to make W computable.
Nicht brechbar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report Umwelt- violations at BSP facilities through Shell's global ethics and compliance channels
Document Emissionen data gaps — report to the Department of Environment, Parks and Recreation
Use BSP's internal reporting mechanisms for safety and Umwelt- concerns
BSP/Shell: Extend Shell's global net-zero commitments to Brunei operations

Leverage Brunei's ASEAN chairmanship rotations to advance regional climate commitments
i: BSP (Brunei Shell Petroleum) — a 50/50 joint venture between the government of Brunei and Shell, the country's largest oil and gas producer. BSP operates onshore and offshore fields. Department of Environment, Parks and Recreation — under the Ministry of Entwickelnment, responsible for Umwelt- policy and regulation. Note: Brunei is an absolute monarchy with no elected legislature. There is no Hinweisgeber protection legislation. Reporting channels are primarily internal corporate mechanisms.

BSP (Brunei Shell Petroleum) — managed jointly by the Brunei government and Shell. BIA (Brunei Investment Agency) — the sovereign wealth fund managing Brunei's petroleum revenues (estimated $30-40B). Total E&P Borneo — operates deep water gas fields offshore Brunei. PetroleumBRUNEI — the national petroleum authority overseeing the sector.

PetroleumBRUNEI — the national petroleum authority responsible for policy, regulation, and management of Brunei's oil and gas resources. AMBD (Autoriti Monetari Brunei Darussalam) — Brunei's central bank and financial regulator. Department of Energy — under the Prime Minister's Office, coordinates energy policy and the Wawasan 2035 energy diversification strategy.

Note: Brunei is an absolute monarchy (Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah). There is no elected legislature — the Legislative Council (Majlis Mesyuarat Negara) is appointed and has advisory functions only. Civic engagement operates through petition to the Sultan and through ASEAN and international multilateral channels. ASEAN — Brunei is a founding member. ASEAN's Framework for Circular Economy and declaration on Klimawandel provide regional engagement mechanisms.

BO.s: Resource-dependent Wirtschaft ($54.9B BIP) with significant natural gas reserves (gas=1.85% rents) and Bergbau (min=5.91%, lithium in Salar de Uyuni — world's largest deposit). Landwirtschaft bei 8.8% BIP with soy expansion driving Entwaldung. Gas production declining from mature fields.

sectors.w: Bolivia holds the world's largest lithium reserves in the Salar de Uyuni. Lithium extraction uses massive water volumes in arid regions. Wenn water consumption and brine chemistry data are published alongside production revenue, W wird berechenbar.
bp: Require community benefit agreements and water rights guarantees for all lithium extraction. Wenn indigenous communities (who hold constitutional land rights under Bolivia's 2009 Constitution) can negotiate fair terms, das überlappende Interesse an extraction without consent wird geschwächt.
Mandatory publication of water extraction volumes, brine chemistry changes, and downstream ecosystem data per lithium operation. Making Umwelt- costs visible makes W computable from production data.

Teilweise brechbar. Lithium brine extraction inherently alters aquifer chemistry and reduces water availability in arid ecosystems. However, direct lithium extraction (DLE) technology can significantly reduce water consumption compared to evaporation ponds.
a: Report Umwelt- violations at Bergbau operations to the Autoridad de Fiscalización y Control Social de Bosques y Tierra (ABT)
Document water contamination or indigenous rights violations — report to the Defensoría del Pueblo
Report safety violations at Bergbau sites to the Ministry of Labor

Übernehmen international sustainability standards (IRMA — Initiative for Responsible Mining Assurance)

MMAyA (Ministry of Environment and Water): Durchsetzen water extraction limits in Salar de Uyuni basin
AJAM: Require Umwelt- bonds for all Bergbau concessions

Support ILO Convention 169 enforcement on indigenous consultation rights in extractive industries
Advocate for international standards on sustainable lithium extraction through the Lithium Triangle coordination (Bolivia, Argentina, Chile)
Drängen auf inclusion of water depletion costs in Bergbau revenue accounting
i: AJAM (Autoridad Jurisdiccional Administrativa Minera) — the Bergbau administrative authority responsible for granting and overseeing Bergbau concessions. Defensoría del Pueblo — Bolivia's human rights ombudsman, responsible for investigating complaints about government actions affecting fundamental rights, including indigenous and Umwelt- rights. Bolivia's 2009 Constitution (Article 30) — guarantees indigenous peoples' right to prior consultation on resource extraction in their territories.

YLB (Yacimientos de Litio Bolivianos) — Bolivia's state lithium company, managing lithium industrialization in the Salar de Uyuni. COMIBOL (Corporación Minera de Bolivia) — the state Bergbau corporation overseeing Bolivia's Bergbau sector. YPFB (Yacimientos Petrolíferos Fiscales Bolivianos) — the state hydrocarbons company.

Ministerio de Minería y Metalurgia — Bolivia's Bergbau ministry, responsible for Bergbau policy and regulation. MMAyA (Ministerio de Medio Ambiente y Agua) — the environment and water ministry, overseeing Umwelt- policy and water resource management. SENARECOM (Servicio Nacional de Registro y Control de la Comercialización de Minerales y Metales) — the national service for mineral commerce registration and control.

Asamblea Legislativa Plurinacional (Plurinational Legislative Assembly) — Bolivia's bicameral parliament consisting of the Cámara de Diputados (130 members) and the Cámara de Senadores (36 members). Bolivia's 2009 Constitution establishes a 'plurinational' state recognizing indigenous rights and communitarian Demokratie. ILO Convention 169 — Bolivia has ratified ILO Convention 169 on Indigenous and Tribal Peoples, requiring free, prior, and informed consultation on resource extraction in indigenous territories.

BS.s: Small island Wirtschaft ($15.8B BIP) dominated by tourism and financial services (77.2% services). Extreme climate vulnerability — low-lying islands face existential risk from sea level rise and intensifying hurricanes (Hurricane Dorian, 2019, caused $3.4B damage — 25% of BIP).

sectors.w: The Bahamas' offshore financial center enables capital flows that may avoid tax obligations elsewhere. Wenn beneficial ownership information and tax Transparenz data are published, the system welfare impact of offshore finance becomes computable.
bp: Umsetzen full beneficial ownership Transparenz in line with FATF recommendations. Wenn the competitive advantage of Bahamian financial secrecy is eliminated through Transparenz, the überlappendes Interesse between offshore service providers and tax-avoiding clients wird geschwächt.

Publish aggregate data on capital flows, tax rulings, and beneficial ownership through enhanced OECD Common Reporting Standard participation. Making tax revenue impacts visible to source jurisdictions makes W computable.
Teilweise brechbar. Financial intermediation can relocate — but the Bahamas' geographic proximity to the US and established legal infrastructure create switching costs that give regulation leverage.
a: Report suspicious transactions to the Financial Intelligence Unit (FIU) of The Bahamas
Document beneficial ownership concealment — report to the Securities Commission of The Bahamas
Report regulatory violations to the Central Bank of The Bahamas

Umsetzen beneficial ownership Transparenz standards ahead of regulatory mandates
Integrate climate risk assessment into financial products and client advisory services

Securities Commission: Durchsetzen FATF recommendations on Transparenz and anti-money laundering
Compliance Commission: Increase supervision of designated non-financial businesses and professions
Kontaktieren Sie Ihren Member of Parliament in the House of Assembly um zu fordern enhanced financial Transparenz legislation
Support OECD Base Erosion and Profit Shifting (BEPS) Inclusive Framework participation
Leverage the Alliance of Small Island States (AOSIS) to link climate finance to financial Transparenz commitments
Advocate for Caribbean regional coordination on beneficial ownership Transparenz
i: FIU (Financial Intelligence Unit of The Bahamas) — the agency responsible for receiving, analyzing, and disseminating suspicious transaction reports. The FIU works with law enforcement to combat money laundering and terrorist financing. Securities Commission of The Bahamas — regulates the investment funds industry and securities market. Central Bank of The Bahamas — the prudential regulator of banks and trust companies.

The Bahamas is home to approximately 200 licensed banks and trust companies. Major international banks maintain operations in Nassau. The Bahamas Finanzdienstleistungen Board (BFSB) promotes the financial services sector and coordinates industry standards.

Central Bank of The Bahamas — the primary financial regulator and supervisor. The Compliance Commission — supervises non-bank financial service providers for anti-money laundering compliance. FATF (Financial Action Task Force) — The Bahamas has been subject to FATF enhanced monitoring and has implemented reforms to address identified deficiencies in its AML/CFT framework.

Parliament of The Bahamas — bicameral legislature consisting of the House of Assembly (39 members) and the Senate (16 appointed members). Citizens can contact their MP through the official government portal. The Bahamas is a Westminster-style parlamentarisch Demokratie. AOSIS (Alliance of Small Island States) — The Bahamas is an active member, advocating for climate action and sea level rise mitigation. CARICOM — provides regional coordination on financial regulation.

BW.s: Upper-middle-income Wirtschaft ($19.4B BIP) with diamond Bergbau historically driving growth (Debswana JV with De Beers). Services-dominant (63.5% BIP). One of Africa's most stable democracies. Diversification challenge as lab-grown diamonds threaten natural diamond demand.

sectors.w: Lab-grown diamonds are disrupting the natural diamond market (30%+ of gem market by volume). As synthetic alternatives become indistinguishable and cheaper, the überlappendes Interesse between Debswana and consumers in natural diamond premium wird geschwächt — Botswana must diversify.
bp: Accelerate economic diversification using Pula Fund sovereign wealth. Wenn non-diamond revenue streams (tech, tourism, beef) exceed declining diamond revenues, das überlappende Interesse an continued diamond dependence breaks naturally.

Publish full lifecycle Umwelt- data (water use, land disturbance, energy consumption) per carat for all Bergbau operations. Making Umwelt- costs visible makes W computable from diamond production data.

Teilweise brechbar. Open-pit diamond Bergbau inherently disturbs landscapes. However, Botswana's mines are in relatively low-biodiversity Kalahari terrain, and rehabilitation programs can reduce long-term Systemabhängigkeit.
a: Report Umwelt- violations at mine sites to DEA (Department of Environmental Affairs)
Document community displacement or inadequate compensation — report to the Ombudsman
Report safety violations to the Department of Mines
Debswana: Entwickeln post-diamond economic transition plan with erneuerbare Energie investment
De Beers: Extend lifecycle Umwelt- reporting to all Botswana operations
Übernehmen Kimberley Process Certification Scheme enhanced standards including Umwelt- metrics
Ministry of Minerals and Energy: Require Umwelt- bonds for all Bergbau licenses
BURS (Botswana Unified Revenue Service): Ensure mineral royalties reflect true Umwelt- costs
Okavango Diamond Company: Lead on sustainability certification for Botswana diamonds

Advocate for Kimberley Process reform to include Umwelt- and social standards

i: DEA (Department of Environmental Affairs) — under the Ministry of Environment and Tourism, responsible for Umwelt- policy, impact assessment, and monitoring. Department of Mines — under the Ministry of Minerals and Energy, responsible for Bergbau safety regulation, licensing, and compliance monitoring. Office of the Ombudsman — investigates complaints against government agencies and public officials.

Debswana Diamond Company — a 50/50 joint venture between the Government of Botswana and De Beers Group, operating the Jwaneng and Orapa mines. Debswana is Botswana's largest private employer and the largest contributor to government revenue. De Beers Group (Anglo American subsidiary) — the global diamond company partnering with Botswana. The 2023 renegotiation of the Debswana agreement gave Botswana a greater share of rough diamond sales.

Ministry of Minerals and Energy — oversees Bergbau policy and the regulatory framework under the Mines and Minerals Act. BURS (Botswana Unified Revenue Service) — the tax authority responsible for collecting mineral royalties and taxes. Pula Fund — Botswana's sovereign wealth fund (managed by the Bank of Botswana), designed to save diamond revenues for future generations.

National Assembly of Botswana — unicameral parliament with 65 members (57 elected, 8 specially elected). Citizens can contact their MPs through the parlamentarisch website. Botswana is one of Africa's most stable multiparty democracies, with peaceful transfers of power since independence in 1966. The Ntlo ya Dikgosi (House of Chiefs) — an advisory body representing traditional leaders, with input on matters affecting tribal territories including Bergbau.

BY.s: State-controlled Wirtschaft ($76B BIP) with significant manufacturing (20.3% BIP). Potash Bergbau (Belaruskali is one of the world's largest producers). Oil refining (Naftan, Mozyr) processes Russian crude. Authoritarian governance under Lukashenko since 1994.

sectors.w: Belarus is the world's third-largest potash producer (Belaruskali). Potash Bergbau generates massive salt waste (solukhards) that contaminate groundwater and rivers. Wenn downstream water contamination data is published alongside potash production revenue, W wird berechenbar.
bp: International Sanktionen (EU, US, UK since 2021) have already weakened the überlappendes Interesse by restricting Belaruskali's market access. Wenn sanctioned export revenues fall below Umwelt- remediation costs, the economic case for continued expansion breaks.

Satellite-based Umwelt- monitoring (salt waste accumulation, water contamination plumes) published by international organizations. Making Umwelt- damage visible from space bypasses domestic data suppression and makes W computable.
Teilweise brechbar. Potash Bergbau inherently generates salt waste. However, solution Bergbau and backfill techniques can reduce surface waste accumulation and groundwater contamination.
a: Document Umwelt- violations through secure channels to international Umwelt- organizations
Report safety concerns through ILO complaint mechanisms
Provide data to international monitoring organizations (Belarusian diaspora networks)

Apply IFC Performance Standards to any future investment in Belarusian Bergbau
Require independent Umwelt- audits as part of off-take agreements
International Sanktionen bodies: Maintain and strengthen potash export Sanktionen until Umwelt- and governance reforms are implemented
UNECE: Continue Umwelt- performance reviews of Belarus under the Convention on Environmental Impact Assessment (Espoo Convention)

Support maintenance of international Sanktionen on Belaruskali until Umwelt- and governance reforms are implemented

i: Note: Belarus under Lukashenko is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. Independent media, Zivilgesellschaft, and trade unions face severe repression. There is no effective Hinweisgeber protection. After the 2020 protests, thousands were imprisoned and major independent organizations were forcibly liquidated. Safe reporting channels include the Belarusian diaspora network, international trade union federations (IndustriALL Global Union), and international Umwelt- organizations.

Belaruskali — Belarus's staatliches potash company, one of the world's largest potash producers. Belaruskali operates six mines near Soligorsk. The company has been under EU, US, and UK Sanktionen since 2021 following the forced diversion of Ryanair Flight 4978 and repression of democratic opposition. BPC (Belarusian Potash Company) — the former exclusive export trading arm of Belaruskali (sanctioned). Competitors include Nutrien (Canada), Mosaic (US), and K+S (Germany).

Note: Domestic regulatory institutions (Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Protection) are not independent under autoritär governance. International mechanisms include: UNECE (United Nations Economic Commission for Europe) — Belarus is party to the Espoo Convention and the Convention on the Protection and Use of Transboundary Watercourses. EU Sanktionen framework — Council Regulation (EU) restricting trade with Belaruskali and associated entities.

Note: Belarus has no functioning democratic legislature. The National Assembly (Natsionalnoye Sobranie) is not freely elected. International engagement channels: EU Eastern Partnership framework, OSCE (Organization for Security and Co-operation in Europe), United Nations Human Rights Council Universal Periodic Review. The Belarusian democratic opposition in exile (led by Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya) maintains a platform including Umwelt- governance reform.

CD.s: Resource-rich but impoverished Wirtschaft ($71B BIP). Mining dominates (28.81% of BIP) — world's largest cobalt producer (70%+ global supply), significant copper, coltan, and tin. Conflict minerals remain a governance challenge. Agricultural employment is 60%+ but only 9.6% of BIP.

sectors.w: DRC's cobalt is essential for EV batteries — downstream manufacturers (Tesla, Samsung SDI, CATL) face increasing ESG pressure. Wenn supply chain traceability makes mine-level Umwelt- and labor conditions visible to end consumers, W wird berechenbar from cobalt prices.
bp: Mandatory supply chain due diligence (EU Battery Regulation, US Dodd-Frank Section 1502) that requires full traceability to mine site. Wenn artisanal miners must meet labor and Umwelt- standards to access global markets, das überlappende Interesse an unregulated extraction wird geschwächt.

Blockchain or RMI-style traceability systems linking pro Mine Umwelt- and labor data to each cobalt lot. The EU Battery Regulation (effective 2027) will require carbon footprint declarations and due diligence — making W computable per unit of cobalt.

Teilweise brechbar. Mining inherently disturbs landscapes and generates waste. However, formalized artisanal Bergbau with proper safety and Umwelt- management can reduce Systemabhängigkeit compared to unregulated extraction.
a: Report Umwelt- or labor violations at mine sites to international monitoring organizations (IPIS, Global Witness)
Document child labor or unsafe working conditions — report through responsible sourcing audit channels (RMI, LBMA)
Report illegal mineral exports to CEEC (Centre d'Évaluation, d'Expertise et de Certification des substances minérales)
Glencore, CMOC (China Moly): Umsetzen full supply chain traceability to mine site for all DRC cobalt
Übernehmen IRMA (Initiative for Responsible Mining Assurance) certification for industrial operations
Investieren in formalization programs for artisanal Bergbau communities
Ministry of Mines: Durchsetzen 2018 Mining Code Umwelt- provisions including community development obligations
CEEC: Stärken mineral certification to include Umwelt- and labor compliance data
Cellule Technique de Coordination et de Planification Minière (CTCPM): Improve artisanal Bergbau registration and oversight

Support EU Battery Regulation implementation requiring full cobalt supply chain traceability
Eintreten über the African Union for continental responsible Bergbau standards
Drängen auf OECD Due Diligence Guidance enforcement for all minerals sourced from DRC
i: CEEC (Centre d'Évaluation, d'Expertise et de Certification des substances minérales précieuses et semi-précieuses) — the DRC government agency responsible for evaluating, certifying, and controlling mineral exports. SAEMAPE (Service d'Assistance et d'Encadrement du Small Scale Mining) — the agency responsible for regulating artisanal and small-scale Bergbau. IPIS (International Peace Information Service) — an independent research institute mapping Bergbau sites, armed group presence, and supply chain risks in the DRC. Global Witness — international NGO investigating the links between natural resource exploitation and conflict.

Glencore — operates Mutanda and Katanga Mining (KCC) copper-cobalt mines, among the world's largest. CMOC (China Molybdenum Co.) — operates Tenke Fungurume, one of the world's largest copper-cobalt mines. Entreprise Générale du Cobalt (EGC) — state-backed enterprise intended to monopolize artisanal cobalt purchasing (established 2019). RMI (Responsible Minerals Initiative) — industry-led supply chain due diligence program used by electronics and automotive manufacturers.

Ministry of Mines — oversees Bergbau policy, licensing, and the 2018 Mining Code (Code Minier), which increased royalty rates and introduced strategic substance classification for cobalt. CTCPM (Cellule Technique de Coordination et de Planification Minière) — the technical coordination unit for Bergbau sector planning. ITSCI (ITRI Tin Supply Chain Initiative) — a traceability program for tin, tantalum, and tungsten (3T) minerals from the DRC.

Parlement de la République démocratique du Congo — bicameral legislature consisting of the Assemblée nationale (500 members) and the Sénat (108 members). The DRC is a semi-presidential republic but governance capacity is limited outside major urban centers. EU Battery Regulation (2023/1542) — effective 2027, requires carbon footprint declarations, recycled content minimums, and supply chain due diligence for batteries containing cobalt. OECD Due Diligence Guidance for Responsible Supply Chains of Minerals from Conflict-Affected and High-Risk Areas — the international standard for mineral supply chain responsibility.

CG.s: Oil-dependent Wirtschaft ($15.7B BIP) with 34.74% combined oil and gas rents — one of Sub-Saharan Africa's highest. TotalEnergies and Eni are major operators. Governance challenges with long-ruling president (Sassou Nguesso since 1997). Significant tropical forest cover.

sectors.w: Congo-Brazzaville's oil production is in long-term decline from mature offshore fields. As extraction costs rise and reserves deplete, the überlappendes Interesse between international operators and government revenue wird geschwächt — diversification becomes necessary.
bp: Redirect oil revenues to economic diversification (forestry, agriculture, services) through transparent budget allocation. Wenn non-oil sectors generate more employment and revenue, das überlappende Interesse an continued extraction from depleting fields breaks.
Umsetzen EITI-standard per-block revenue and Emissionen disclosure. Congo joined EITI in 2004 — extending to Umwelt- data makes W computable per production unit.
Nicht brechbar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report Umwelt- violations at oil facilities to the Ministry of Environment
Document revenue discrepancies — report to EITI Congo multi-stakeholder group
Report concerns to international operator compliance channels (TotalEnergies, Eni)
TotalEnergies, Eni: Umsetzen Paris-aligned transition plans for Congo operations
SNPC: Entwickeln economic diversification strategy using petroleum revenues
Übernehmen per-block Emissionen reporting standards
Ministry of Hydrocarbons: Stärken Umwelt- conditions in production sharing contracts
EITI Congo: Expand reporting to include Umwelt- impact data
Central Bank (BEAC): Integrate resource revenue volatility risk into macroprudential supervision
Eintreten über CEMAC for regional Umwelt- standards in oil and gas extraction
Support EITI enhanced reporting with Umwelt- metrics
Drängen auf Central African Forest Initiative (CAFI) enforcement on forest protection as alternative revenue source
Leverage UNFCCC mechanisms for fossil fuel transition support
i: Ministère de l'Environnement, du Développement Durable et du Bassin du Congo — the environment ministry. SNPC (Société Nationale des Pétroles du Congo) — the national oil company. EITI Congo — the Republic of Congo has been an EITI member since 2004. Note: Whistleblower protections are minimal under current governance.

TotalEnergies — the largest oil operator in Congo-Brazzaville, operating the Moho Nord deepwater project and other fields. Eni — operates significant offshore concessions. SNPC (Société Nationale des Pétroles du Congo) — the national oil company holding state equity in all production sharing contracts.

Ministère des Hydrocarbures — oversees oil and gas policy and licensing. BEAC (Banque des États de l'Afrique Centrale) — the central bank of the CEMAC monetary zone (shared with Cameroon, CAR, Chad, Equatorial Guinea, Gabon). EITI Congo — publishes annual reports on extractive industry revenues.

Note: Congo-Brazzaville under President Sassou Nguesso is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. The Assemblée nationale (151 members) and Sénat (72 members) do not function as independent democratic institutions. International engagement channels: CEMAC (Communauté Économique et Monétaire de l'Afrique Centrale), African Union, CAFI (Central African Forest Initiative) — a partnership between Central African countries and donors to protect tropical forests.

CI.s: Largest Wirtschaft in francophone West Africa ($87.1B BIP). World's largest cocoa producer (~45% of global supply). Agriculture is 15.9% of BIP with cocoa, coffee, and palm oil as main cash crops. Growing oil production (oil=0.7%). Rapid economic growth (~7% annual average).

sectors.w: Côte d'Ivoire's cocoa monoculture has driven 80%+ of original forest loss. EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) will require Entwaldung-free supply chains — when per-farm Entwaldung data is linked to cocoa transactions, W wird berechenbar.
bp: EU Deforestation Regulation compliance forces traceability to farm level. Wenn cocoa buyers must verify zero-Entwaldung, the überlappendes Interesse between traders and smallholders in clearing forest for new plantations breaks — agroforestry becomes the viable model.

Mandatory per-farm Umwelt- data (tree cover, soil carbon, water use) linked to cocoa lot traceability. The Cocoa and Forests Initiative already creates a framework — extending to mandatory data makes W computable from cocoa prices.

Teilweise brechbar. Cocoa monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity compared to native forest. However, cocoa agroforestry systems can maintain significant tree cover and biodiversity — Côte d'Ivoire's National REDD+ Strategy includes agroforestry targets.
a: Report illegal Entwaldung for cocoa expansion to SODEFOR (Société de Développement des Forêts) or OIPR (Office Ivoirien des Parcs et Réserves)
Document child labor in cocoa production — report to CNS (Comité National de Surveillance des actions de lutte contre le travail des enfants)
Report pesticide misuse — notify ANADER (Agence Nationale d'Appui au Développement Rural)
Cocoa traders (Cargill, Barry Callebaut, Olam): Umsetzen full farm-level traceability ahead of EUDR deadline
Investieren in cocoa agroforestry systems that maintain tree cover while improving yields
Übernehmen living income reference price commitments for cocoa farmers
CCC (Conseil du Café-Cacao): Durchsetzen traceability requirements and link farmgate prices to Umwelt- compliance
MINEDD (Ministry of Environment): Stärken enforcement against Entwaldung in classified forests
ANADER: Scale agroforestry extension programs to all cocoa-growing regions
Kontaktieren Sie Ihren Député in the Assemblée nationale um zu fordern enforcement of the Forest Code and protection of classified forests
Support EU Deforestation Regulation implementation with adequate transition support for smallholder farmers
Eintreten über the Cocoa and Forests Initiative for binding Entwaldung targets
Drängen auf living income benchmarks in international cocoa pricing through ICCO
i: SODEFOR (Société de Développement des Forêts) — the state forestry company responsible for managing classified forests, many of which have been illegally cleared for cocoa. OIPR (Office Ivoirien des Parcs et Réserves) — manages national parks and protected areas. CNS (Comité National de Surveillance) — the national committee monitoring child labor in cocoa, coordinated with the International Cocoa Initiative (ICI). ANADER — the national rural development support agency providing agricultural extension services.

Major cocoa traders operating in Côte d'Ivoire: Cargill, Barry Callebaut, Olam International, Sucden. These companies intermediate the majority of Ivorian cocoa exports. Cocoa and Forests Initiative (CFI) — a public-private partnership between cocoa-producing countries (Côte d'Ivoire, Ghana) and leading chocolate companies to end Entwaldung in cocoa supply chains. The Conseil du Café-Cacao (CCC) — the Ivorian cocoa board that sets farmgate prices and regulates the sector.

CCC (Conseil du Café-Cacao) — the government body regulating Côte d'Ivoire's cocoa and coffee sectors, setting minimum farmgate prices, managing quality standards, and overseeing exports. MINEDD (Ministère de l'Environnement et du Développement Durable) — the environment ministry responsible for forest protection, climate policy, and Umwelt- regulation.

Assemblée nationale de Côte d'Ivoire — unicameral parliament with 255 members. Citizens can contact deputies through the official Assembly website. Sénat — the upper house (99 members, established 2020). ICCO (International Cocoa Organization) — the intergovernmental body coordinating global cocoa policy, headquartered in Abidjan. EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR, 2023/1115) — requires EU importers to verify that commodities including cocoa are not linked to Entwaldung after December 2020.

CL.s: Upper-middle-income Wirtschaft ($330.3B BIP) with massive Bergbau sector (16.23% of BIP). World's largest copper producer (~27% of global supply) and second-largest lithium producer. Strong institutions and democratic governance. Renewable energy leader in Latin America (solar in Atacama).

sectors.w: Chile's copper and lithium Bergbau depletes water resources in the Atacama Desert (one of Earth's driest regions). Wenn pro Mine water consumption data is published alongside production revenue, W wird berechenbar from Bergbau output data.
bp: Umsetzen water pricing that reflects true scarcity in Bergbau regions. Wenn miners pay the real cost of water extraction in hyper-arid zones, das überlappende Interesse an water-intensive extraction methods wird geschwächt — desalination and dry processing become economically preferred.

Mandatory pro Mine water balance, tailings chemistry, and Emissionen disclosure. Chile's SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente) already collects Umwelt- data — making it publicly accessible per mine makes W computable.
Teilweise brechbar. Mining inherently disturbs landscapes. However, dry processing, desalination, and tailings reprocessing can significantly reduce water dependence and waste generation.
a: Report Umwelt- violations to SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente)
Document water rights violations — report to DGA (Dirección General de Aguas)
Use Chile's Hinweisgeber law (Ley 21.015) and Umwelt- complaint channels (denuncia ambiental) for protected reporting

Übernehmen Science Based Targets for Emissionen reduction across Bergbau operations

Support the Escazú Agreement on Umwelt- access rights in Latin America (Chile has not ratified)
Advocate for OECD Due Diligence Guidance enforcement for Bergbau supply chains

i: SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente) — Chile's Umwelt- enforcement agency, responsible for monitoring compliance with Umwelt- permits (RCA — Resolución de Calificación Ambiental). SMA can impose fines up to $8.2M and order facility closure. DGA (Dirección General de Aguas) — the national water authority managing water rights and monitoring extraction. SEA (Servicio de Evaluación Ambiental) — conducts Umwelt- impact assessments for Bergbau projects.

CODELCO (Corporación Nacional del Cobre) — Chile's staatliches copper company and the world's largest copper producer. CODELCO operates Chuquicamata, El Teniente, and other major mines. SQM (Sociedad Química y Minera de Chile) — one of the world's largest lithium producers, operating in the Salar de Atacama. Albemarle — US company operating lithium extraction in the Salar de Atacama alongside SQM. BHP, Anglo American — major international copper Bergbau companies operating in Chile.

Sernageomin (Servicio Nacional de Geología y Minería) — the national geological and Bergbau service, responsible for mine safety, tailings dam oversight, and geological surveys. Chile has over 740 tailings facilities, the largest inventory in the world. Ministry of Mining — oversees Bergbau policy including the 2023 National Lithium Strategy, which established state-led lithium development through public-private partnerships.

Congreso Nacional de Chile — bicameral legislature consisting of the Cámara de Diputadas y Diputados (155 members) and the Senado (50 members). Citizens can contact representatives through camara.cl and senado.cl. Chile is an OECD member with strong democratic institutions. Escazú Agreement — the Regional Agreement on Access to Information, Public Participation and Justice in Environmental Matters in Latin America and the Caribbean. Chile signed but has not ratified.

CM.s: Central African Wirtschaft ($53.3B BIP) with oil production (2.94% combined rents) and significant agriculture (18.5% BIP — cocoa, palm oil, timber). Forest cover declining due to logging and agricultural expansion. Governance challenges and Anglophone crisis.

sectors.w: Cameroon's oil production is declining from mature fields. As extraction costs rise, the überlappendes Interesse between operators (Perenco, Addax) and government revenue wird geschwächt — diversification to sustainable forestry and agriculture becomes economically rational.
bp: Redirect declining oil revenues to sustainable forest management and agricultural diversification. Cameroon's forests are a carbon sink worth more standing than cleared. Wenn REDD+ payments exceed marginal oil revenue, das überlappende Interesse an continued extraction breaks.
Umsetzen EITI-standard per-block Umwelt- disclosure. Cameroon's EITI membership provides the framework — extending to Umwelt- data makes W computable.
Nicht brechbar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report Umwelt- violations to MINEPDED (Ministry of Environment, Nature Protection and Sustainable Entwickelnment)
Document illegal logging or land seizures — report to international forestry monitoring organizations (Global Forest Watch)

i: MINEPDED (Ministère de l'Environnement, de la Protection de la Nature et du Développement Durable) — Cameroon's environment ministry. SNH (Société Nationale des Hydrocarbures) — the national hydrocarbons company managing state interests in oil and gas. EITI Cameroon — member since 2007. Note: Whistleblower protections are limited.

Perenco — the largest oil producer in Cameroon. Addax Petroleum (Sinopec subsidiary) — operates offshore concessions. SNH manages the state's petroleum interests and holds equity in production sharing contracts.

MINMIDT (Ministère des Mines, de l'Industrie et du Développement Technologique) — oversees Bergbau and industrial policy. CONAC (Commission Nationale Anti-Corruption) — Cameroon's anti-corruption commission.

Assemblée nationale du Cameroun — 180 members. Sénat — 100 members. Note: Cameroon's democratic institutions are constrained under long-ruling President Paul Biya (in power since 1982). International channels: CEMAC, African Union, CAFI.

CO.s: Major Latin American Wirtschaft ($418.8B BIP) with significant oil (3.42% rents) and coal (0.73% rents) production. Ecopetrol is the largest company. Biodiversity hotspot (most biodiverse country per square kilometer). 2016 peace agreement with FARC opened new governance challenges in former conflict zones.

sectors.w: Colombia's current administration (Petro, 2022-) has announced a policy of no new oil exploration licenses. If maintained, das überlappende Interesse an fossil fuel expansion breaks as existing fields deplete. Colombia's renewable potential (hydro, solar, wind) provides viable alternatives.
bp: Maintain and implement the no-new-exploration-licenses policy while investing transition revenues in erneuerbare Energie. Wenn energy investment flows shift from fossil to renewable, das überlappende Interesse an continued exploration breaks.

Mandatory pro Feld Emissionen disclosure for all Ecopetrol and international operator fields. Colombia's ANH (Agencia Nacional de Hidrocarburos) already collects production data — extending to Emissionen makes W computable.
Nicht brechbar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report Umwelt- violations to ANLA (Autoridad Nacional de Licencias Ambientales)
Document community health impacts near oil operations — report to Defensoría del Pueblo

Ecopetrol: Accelerate energy transition strategy with binding renewable targets

Stärken Colombia's NDC with binding sectoral Emissionen reduction targets
Leverage OECD membership to advance Umwelt- governance standards
Support the Leticia Pact for Amazon conservation as alternative economic model
i: ANLA (Autoridad Nacional de Licencias Ambientales) — Colombia's Umwelt- licensing authority, responsible for Umwelt- impact assessment and compliance monitoring for extractive projects. Defensoría del Pueblo — the national human rights ombudsman. Contraloría General de la República — the national audit office overseeing public resource management including oil revenues.

Ecopetrol — Colombia's largest company (88.5% staatliches), responsible for most domestic oil production. Ecopetrol has announced energy transition targets but continues significant upstream investment. ANH (Agencia Nacional de Hidrocarburos) — manages exploration and production contracts.

MinAmbiente (Ministerio de Ambiente y Desarrollo Sostenible) — Colombia's environment ministry. CREG (Comisión de Regulación de Energía y Gas) — the Energieregulierungsbehördey commission. CAR (Corporaciones Autónomas Regionales) — regional Umwelt- authorities.

Congreso de la República de Colombia — bicameral legislature: Cámara de Representantes (188 members) and Senado (108 members). Contact through camara.gov.co and senado.gov.co. Colombia is an OECD member (2020) with strong democratic institutions. The 2016 Peace Agreement (Acuerdo de Paz) created new governance frameworks for former conflict zones that intersect with extractive industry regulation.

CR.s: Upper-middle-income Wirtschaft ($95.4B BIP) with 98%+ renewable electricity generation. No military since 1948. Services-dominant (68.8% BIP). Leader in ecotourism and Umwelt- policy. Low direct fossil fuel extraction. Main PST exposure through agricultural monoculture (pineapple, banana, palm oil).

sectors.w: Costa Rica's pineapple and banana monoculture uses intensive pesticide application that contaminates waterways. Wenn per-farm pesticide runoff and water contamination data is published alongside export revenue, W wird berechenbar.
bp: Reformieren agricultural export incentives to reward biodiversity-positive farming. Wenn export tax benefits require Umwelt- certification, the überlappendes Interesse between monoculture producers and exporters in chemical-intensive production wird geschwächt.

Mandatory per-farm water quality monitoring downstream of monoculture operations. Costa Rica's AyA (water utility) and SINAC already collect water data — linking to agricultural output makes W computable.
Teilweise brechbar. Tropical monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity. However, Costa Rica's Payment for Environmental Services (PSA) program demonstrates viable alternatives.
a: Report pesticide violations to SFE (Servicio Fitosanitario del Estado)
Document water contamination — report to MINAE (Ministry of Environment) or AyA
Use Costa Rica's labor protections and Umwelt- complaint mechanisms

Support the Escazú Agreement implementation (Costa Rica was an early advocate)
Leverage Costa Rica's Umwelt- reputation to advance international agricultural sustainability standards
Drängen auf green trade provisions in bilateral agreements
i: SFE (Servicio Fitosanitario del Estado) — the state phytosanitary service regulating pesticide use. MINAE (Ministerio de Ambiente y Energía) — the environment and energy ministry. AyA (Instituto Costarricense de Acueductos y Alcantarillados) — the national water utility. SETENA (Secretaría Técnica Nacional Ambiental) — conducts Umwelt- impact assessments.

Costa Rica hosts operations of major fruit multinationals: Del Monte, Dole, Chiquita. CORBANA (Corporación Bananera Nacional) — the national banana corporation coordinating the sector. CANAPEP (Cámara Nacional de Productores y Exportadores de Piña) — the pineapple producers' association.

MAG (Ministerio de Agricultura y Ganadería) — Costa Rica's agriculture ministry. The PSA (Pago por Servicios Ambientales) program — Costa Rica's pioneering payment for Umwelt- services program, which pays landowners to maintain forest cover, funded partly by a fuel tax.

Asamblea Legislativa de Costa Rica — unicameral parliament with 57 members. Citizens can contact representatives through asamblea.go.cr. Costa Rica is one of Latin America's strongest democracies and a global Umwelt- leader. The Escazú Agreement — Costa Rica was one of the first signatories of this regional agreement on Umwelt- access rights.

CU.s: Command Wirtschaft ($107.4B BIP, though estimates vary due to dual currency system). Services-dominant (73.5% BIP, driven by healthcare, education, tourism). Minimal resource extraction. Economic crisis exacerbated by US embargo and collapse of Venezuelan oil subsidies.

sectors.w: Cuba's sugar industry collapsed after Soviet subsidies ended, but remaining agriculture (state farms, cooperatives) relies on monoculture patterns. Cuba's organic farming necessity (post-Soviet fertilizer shortage) demonstrates alternative models. Making soil health data visible breaks system independence.
bp: Expand the urban agriculture and organoponics model that Cuba developed out of necessity. Wenn organic production meets domestic food needs more reliably than import-dependent monoculture, das überlappende Interesse an conventional agriculture breaks.
Publish soil health, water quality, and biodiversity data from Cuba's extensive agricultural research system. Cuba's scientific institutions (INIFAT, ICA) generate data that could make W computable.
Teilweise brechbar. Cuba's experience with organic farming post-1991 shows that Systemabhängigkeit on chemical inputs can be dramatically reduced through agroecological methods.
a: Document Umwelt- degradation through academic research channels
Report through workplace union structures (CTC — Central de Trabajadores de Cuba)
Engage with international agricultural research partnerships

AZCUBA: Diversify sugar industry toward bioenergy and specialty products
International partners: Support technology transfer for sustainable agriculture

CITMA: Stärken Umwelt- monitoring of agricultural practices
INIFAT: Publish research on agroecological transition outcomes
Support FAO technical cooperation programs for Cuban agricultural sustainability

Support South-South cooperation on agroecological methods developed in Cuba
i: Note: Cuba is a Einparteienstaat. Independent Zivilgesellschaft, media, and labor organizations are not permitted. Reporting channels operate within state structures. CTC (Central de Trabajadores de Cuba) — the state-controlled trade union federation. INIFAT (Instituto Nacional de Investigaciones Fundamentales en Agricultura Tropical) — the national agricultural research institute. CITMA (Ministerio de Ciencia, Tecnología y Medio Ambiente) — the science and environment ministry.

AZCUBA (Empresa Azucarera) — Cuba's state sugar enterprise. State agricultural enterprises and UBPCs (Unidades Básicas de Producción Cooperativa) — cooperatives managing most agricultural production. Cuba's urban agriculture program is one of the world's most successful, producing significant food in cities through organoponics.

MINAG (Ministerio de la Agricultura) — Cuba's agriculture ministry. CITMA — oversees Umwelt- policy and scientific research. The Asociación Nacional de Agricultores Pequeños (ANAP) — the national small farmers' association, which has been a key partner in agroecological transition.

Note: Cuba's Asamblea Nacional del Poder Popular (605 members) is the legislature but functions within a one-party system. International engagement channels: FAO (Cuba has received recognition for reducing hunger), CELAC (Community of Latin American and Caribbean States), NAM (Non-Aligned Movement). Cuba's agroecological experience is studied globally as a model for post-petrochemical agriculture.

CY.s: Small EU island Wirtschaft ($37.6B BIP) with dominant services sector (76.5% BIP, driven by financial services, shipping, and tourism). No significant resource extraction. Post-2013 banking crisis reforms strengthened financial regulation. Division between Republic of Cyprus and northern territory.

sectors.w: Cyprus's financial services sector has historically facilitated capital flows from Russia and other jurisdictions. EU AML directives and beneficial ownership Transparenz requirements make the welfare impact of opaque financial intermediation computable.
bp: Full implementation of EU Anti-Money Laundering Directives and beneficial ownership Transparenz. Wenn financial secrecy is eliminated, the überlappendes Interesse between Cypriot service providers and clients seeking opacity wird geschwächt.

Mandatory publication of Country-by-Country Reporting (CbCR) data for entities registered in Cyprus. EU ATAD and DAC6 frameworks provide the mechanism — making tax planning structures visible breaks system independence.
Teilweise brechbar. Financial intermediation can relocate — but Cyprus's EU-Mitgliedship, common law heritage, and timezone advantages create switching costs.
a: Report suspicious transactions to MOKAS (Unit for Combating Money Laundering)
Document beneficial ownership concealment — report to CySEC (Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission)
Use EU Whistleblower Directive protections (transposed 2022)

Drängen auf EU-wide beneficial ownership registry interconnection
i: MOKAS (Μονάδα Καταπολέμησης Αδικημάτων Συγκάλυψης / Unit for Combating Money Laundering) — Cyprus's financial intelligence unit. CySEC (Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission) — the securities and financial markets regulator. CBC (Central Bank of Cyprus) — the prudential supervisor of banks. Cyprus transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2022.

Cyprus hosts approximately 40 licensed banks and numerous investment firms. The Cyprus Investment Firms sector manages significant cross-border capital flows. CIFA (Cyprus Investment Funds Association) — the industry association representing investment fund managers.

CySEC — responsible for securities regulation and investment firm supervision. CBC (Central Bank of Cyprus) — conducts banking supervision and AML compliance monitoring. ICPAC — the professional body for accountants and auditors in Cyprus.

Βουλή των Αντιπροσώπων (House of Representatives) — unicameral parliament with 56 elected members (80 seats including 24 reserved for Turkish Cypriot community). Contact through parliament.cy. Cyprus is an EU-Mitglied with robust democratic institutions. Moneyval (Council of Europe's Committee of Experts on the Evaluation of Anti-Money Laundering Measures) evaluates Cyprus's AML framework.

CZ.s: Central European industrial Wirtschaft ($347B BIP) with strong manufacturing (19.9% BIP, automotive-dominated). Small coal sector (lignite in northern Bohemia). EU-Mitglied. Significant nuclear energy capacity (Dukovany, Temelín). Dienstleistungen bei 60.2% BIP.

sectors.w: Czechia's lignite Bergbau is concentrated in the Ústí nad Labem region (Sokolovská uhelná, Severní energetická). EU ETS carbon pricing makes Czech lignite uncompetitive — the überlappendes Interesse between mine operators and local employment breaks when carbon costs exceed revenue.
bp: Align with EU ETS carbon pricing and eliminate free allowances for Czech lignite power. Wenn plants pay the full carbon price, lignite electricity becomes more expensive than alternatives and the überlappendes Interesse breaks.
Mandatory pro Anlage Emissionen and health-impact reporting. Czech lignite plants cause significant air pollution — publishing health costs per MWh makes W computable.
Nicht brechbar. Lignite combustion releases CO₂ and pollutants by physical process.
a: Report Emissionen violations to ČIŽP (Czech Environmental Inspectorate)
Document health impacts in Bergbau communities — report to regional public health authorities
Use the Czech Hinweisgeber protection law (zákon o ochraně oznamovatelů, 2023)

Sev.en Energy: Entwickeln mine closure and site remediation plans
Investieren in nuclear energy expansion as baseload alternative
ERÚ: Reformieren electricity tariffs to reflect carbon costs
MŽP: Stärken air quality enforcement in Bergbau regions

Support EU Just Transition Fund allocation for Ústí nad Labem and Moravian-Silesian regions
Advocate for accelerated EU ETS reform

i: ČIŽP (Česká inspekce životního prostředí / Czech Environmental Inspectorate) — the enforcement agency for Umwelt- law, conducting inspections and imposing Sanktionen. MŽP (Ministerstvo životního prostředí / Ministry of the Environment) — oversees Umwelt- policy. Zákon o ochraně oznamovatelů (Act on Protection of Whistleblowers, 2023) — Czech transposition of EU Whistleblower Directive.

ČEZ Group — Czechia's largest energy company (70% staatliches), operating coal, nuclear, and renewable power plants. Sev.en Energy — the largest private lignite Bergbau and power company. Sokolovská uhelná — operates lignite mines in the Sokolov basin.

ERÚ (Energetický regulační úřad / Energy Regulatory Office) — the independent Energieregulierungsbehörde. Czech Coal Commission (Uhelná komise) — established to recommend a coal phase-out date. Recommended 2033, but implementation timelines remain debated.

Parlament České republiky — bicameral: Poslanecká sněmovna (200 members) and Senát (81 members). Contact through psp.cz and senat.cz. EU Just Transition Fund — Czechia is eligible for significant funding for coal-dependent regions.

DK.s: Advanced Nordic Wirtschaft ($424.5B BIP) with global leadership in wind energy (Vestas, Ørsted). Intensive livestock agriculture (28M pigs for 5.8M people) creates significant nitrogen pollution. Dienstleistungen bei 63.5% BIP. World's most ambitious climate law (70% reduction by 2030).

sectors.w: Denmark's intensive pig farming generates nitrogen pollution visible in marine dead zones (inner Danish waters, Baltic Sea). EU Nitrates Directive compliance data makes W computable from farm-level nitrogen balance sheets.
bp: Reformieren agricultural subsidies to penalize nitrogen surplus per hectare. Wenn the überlappendes Interesse between livestock producers and feed importers is broken by internalizing the nitrogen externality, intensive livestock becomes unprofitable without subsidy.

Mandatory farm-level nitrogen and phosphorus balance disclosure. Denmark already has one of Europe's most detailed agricultural data systems — extending to mandatory public disclosure makes W computable.

Teilweise brechbar. Intensive livestock inherently concentrates nutrients beyond ecosystem absorption. However, Denmark's leadership in biogas from manure demonstrates Systemabhängigkeit can be reduced through circular nutrient management.
a: Report nitrogen limit violations to Miljøstyrelsen (Danish Environmental Protection Agency)

i: Miljøstyrelsen — responsible for Umwelt- regulation including agricultural Emissionen and water quality. Fødevarestyrelsen (Danish Veterinary and Food Administration) — oversees food safety and animal welfare. Danish Whistleblower Act (2021) — transposition of EU Directive 2019/1937.
Danish Crown — one of Europe's largest meat processors (farmer cooperative). Arla Foods — multinational dairy cooperative headquartered in Denmark. DLG Group — major agricultural input cooperative.

Miljøministeriet (Ministry of Environment) — Umwelt- policy including agricultural regulation. Landbrugsstyrelsen (Danish Agricultural Agency) — administers CAP payments. The 2021 green agriculture agreement is one of Europe's most ambitious.

Folketinget — unicameral parliament (179 members). Contact through ft.dk. Klimaloven (Climate Act, 2020) — legally binding 70% Emissionen reduction target by 2030. HELCOM (Helsinki Commission) — the governing body of the Convention on the Protection of the Marine Environment of the Baltic Sea Area.

DO.s: Caribbean's largest Wirtschaft ($124.3B BIP) with tourism, manufacturing (free trade zones), and Bergbau (2.04% BIP — Pueblo Viejo gold mine, one of the world's largest). Dienstleistungen bei 59.8% BIP. Climate vulnerable — hurricanes and sea level rise.

sectors.w: Pueblo Viejo gold mine (Barrick Gold) generates cyanide and mercury contamination risks. Wenn pro Mine water quality and health data is published alongside production revenue, W wird berechenbar.
bp: Mandate Umwelt- bonds that fully internalize mine closure and remediation costs. Wenn Bergbau companies pre-fund cleanup, das überlappende Interesse an extraction without Umwelt- accountability wird geschwächt.
Mandatory water quality monitoring and health-impact disclosure per mine. Publishing downstream contamination data makes W computable from Bergbau revenue.
Teilweise brechbar. Open-pit Bergbau disturbs landscapes. However, improved tailings management and water treatment can reduce Systemabhängigkeit.

Document community health impacts — report to health authorities

Support EITI membership for the Dominican Republic

Drängen auf Bergbau revenue Transparenz
i: Ministerio de Medio Ambiente y Recursos Naturales — the environment ministry. Dirección General de Minería — the Bergbau directorate under the Ministry of Energy and Mines. Defensor del Pueblo — the national ombudsman.
Barrick Gold — operates Pueblo Viejo, one of the world's largest gold mines (JV with Newmont). The mine produces ~800,000 ounces of gold annually.
Ministerio de Energía y Minas — oversees Bergbau policy. Ley 146-71 (Mining Law) — governs Bergbau concessions and Umwelt- obligations.
Congreso Nacional — bicameral: Cámara de Diputados (190 members) and Senado (32 members). The Dominican Republic is a presidential Demokratie.

DZ.s: Major oil and gas producer ($269.3B BIP) with 22.46% combined oil and gas rents. Sonatrach is Africa's largest company. Economy heavily dependent on hydrocarbon exports (90%+ of export revenue). Landwirtschaft bei 14% BIP (primarily wheat and dates). Authoritarian governance.

sectors.w: Algeria's oil production is declining from mature Hassi Messaoud and Hassi R'Mel fields. As domestic energy consumption grows (population of 45M+), the exportable surplus shrinks. Wenn production costs exceed export revenue minus domestic consumption, das überlappende Interesse an continued expansion breaks.
bp: Redirect hydrocarbon revenues to solar energy (Algeria has the Sahara — one of Earth's highest solar irradiance zones). Wenn solar electricity is cheaper than burning domestically-consumed gas, das überlappende Interesse an gas-fired power generation breaks.
Umsetzen pro Feld Emissionen disclosure for Sonatrach operations. Algeria's massive gas flaring (visible from satellite) makes Umwelt- damage measurable — publishing this data makes W computable.
Nicht brechbar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.

Document gas flaring data — report to Ministry of Energy and Mines

i: Sonatrach — Algeria's state oil and gas company, Africa's largest company by revenue. Sonatrach controls all upstream and downstream petroleum operations. Note: Algeria has limited press freedom and no comprehensive Hinweisgeber protection. Independent reporting is constrained.
Sonatrach operates the world's longest natural gas pipelines (Transmed to Italy, MEG to Spain). Eni, TotalEnergies, Equinor, Occidental — major international partners in Algerian upstream operations.

ALNAFT (Agence Nationale pour la Valorisation des Ressources en Hydrocarbures) — the hydrocarbons regulatory agency managing licensing. CREG (Commission de Régulation de l'Électricité et du Gaz) — the electricity and gas regulatory commission. ARH (Autorité de Régulation des Hydrocarbures) — the hydrocarbons sector regulator.

Note: Algeria is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. The Assemblée populaire nationale (407 members) and Conseil de la Nation (174 members) do not function as independent democratic institutions. International channels: OPEC membership, Mediterranean dialogue (Union for the Mediterranean), African Union, Arab League. Algeria's strategic gas supply position (supplier to Italy, Spain via pipeline) gives it leverage in energy transition negotiations.

EC.s: Oil-dependent Wirtschaft ($124.7B BIP) with 6.41% oil rents. PetroEcuador manages state interests. Extraordinary biodiversity (Galápagos, Amazon basin). The 2008 Constitution grants 'rights of nature' (Pachamama). Landwirtschaft bei 9.5% BIP (bananas, shrimp, cacao).

sectors.w: Ecuador's 2023 referendum voted to ban oil extraction in Yasuní National Park (one of the world's most biodiverse areas). This democratic decision to forgo extraction directly breaks the überlappendes Interesse. Extending this precedent to other sensitive areas accelerates the transition.
bp: Umsetzen the Yasuní referendum result (ban extraction in ITT block) and extend the precedent to other ecologically sensitive areas. Wenn democratic decisions override extraction interests, the überlappendes Interesse breaks through civic action.

Mandate per-block Umwelt- impact disclosure for all PetroEcuador and private operator fields. Ecuador's Umwelt- data (Amazon Entwaldung, water contamination from legacy Texaco/Chevron operations) makes W measurable.
Nicht brechbar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
